Open the AP opening balance area. Enter a similar prior-period
open vendor invoice for $5,000.
steps.expected: AP entry is created with correct aging.

steps.action: Run an AR aging report and AP aging report.
steps.expected: Both opening balances appear in the correct aging buckets.

expected_overall:
Opening AR and AP balances are entered with correct aging and
appear in standard reports.
pass_criteria: AR and AP totals match what was entered AND aging is correct.

negative_variants.title: Reject opening AR with future invoice date
negative_variants.action:
Enter an opening AR line with original invoice date in the
future.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "invoice date cannot be after
today" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Future-dated opening AR refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject opening AP without vendor invoice number
negative_variants.action:
Try to save an opening AP entry with the original vendor
invoice number blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; opening AP requires the source
invoice reference.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Opening AP without source reference refused.

title: Pay an approved vendor invoice
goal:
Verify the payment workflow — issue a check or ACH for an
approved invoice, post the payment, and clear the AP entry.
preconditions: A vendor invoice has been 3-way matched against its PO and receipt, is approved for payment, and AP is increased accordingly. (Established by P3-AP-001.)

steps.action:
Open the AP payments area. Select the approved Pacific Steel
Supply invoice.
steps.expected: The invoice appears with full amount due.

steps.action: Issue payment by check (or ACH if integrated). Confirm.
steps.expected:
Payment is recorded. Check number / ACH ref is captured.
Invoice is marked paid.

steps.action: Verify GL impact: cash credited, AP debited (cleared).
steps.expected:
GL postings are correct. AP balance for this vendor decreases
by the payment amount.

expected_overall: Vendor payment processed end-to-end. AP cleared.
pass_criteria: Invoice marked paid AND cash and AP postings are correct.

negative_variants.title: Reject payment exceeding invoice balance
negative_variants.action:
Try to record payment of $5,000 on an invoice with a $2,250
balance remaining.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; over-payment requires explicit
handling (credit-on-account) and confirmation.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-payment is gated and explicit.

negative_variants.title: Reject double payment of same invoice
negative_variants.action:
After the invoice is marked paid, try to issue a second
payment for the same invoice.
negative_variants.expected:
The invoice is filtered out of the payable list, or the second
payment is blocked with a "already paid" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Double payment refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject payment from a closed period
negative_variants.action: Set the payment date to a date in a closed accounting period.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "period is closed" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Payment in closed period refused.

title: Run a payment batch covering multiple vendor invoices
goal:
Verify the application supports a batch / payment-run workflow:
select multiple approved vendor invoices, choose a payment method,
generate one set of payments (one check per vendor by default),
and post them as a group.
preconditions: At least three approved vendor invoices exist across at least two vendors.

steps.action:
Find the payment-run area. Filter approved invoices by due date
(e.g., due within 7 days). Select all.
steps.expected:
A payment run is staged with one check / ACH per vendor (sum
of selected invoices for that vendor).

steps.action: Confirm and post the run.
steps.expected:
Payments are issued. Each invoice is marked paid. GL impact:
cash credited, AP debited, in one consolidated transaction set.

steps.action:
Open the AP register. Verify each invoice shows paid and
references the correct check / ACH ID.
steps.expected: All invoices show paid. Cross-references resolve.

expected_overall: Batch payment run pays multiple invoices cleanly.
pass_criteria:
All selected invoices marked paid AND one payment per vendor AND
GL postings tie to the run total.

negative_variants.title: Cannot include held invoices in the batch
negative_variants.action:
Mark one approved invoice as "on hold," then run the batch
filter.
negative_variants.expected:
Held invoices are excluded from the staged run with a clear
"X invoices on hold" indicator.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Held invoices excluded.

negative_variants.title: Cannot post run with insufficient cash account balance
negative_variants.action:
Stage a payment run that exceeds the source bank account's
balance.
negative_variants.expected:
Posting is blocked or warns; overdraft requires explicit
acknowledgement.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Overdraft prevention is in place.

negative_variants.title: Cannot duplicate a payment run already posted
negative_variants.action: After posting, attempt to re-post the same run.
negative_variants.expected: The action is unavailable; the posted run cannot be re-issued.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Re-post refused.

title: Run a full physical inventory count and reconcile to system on-hand
goal:
Verify the application supports a full physical inventory: freeze
movement, count every part, capture variances, post adjustments to
reconcile system on-hand with physical reality.
preconditions: At least 5 parts have on-hand inventory.

steps.action:
Find the physical inventory area. Initiate a new physical count
with a count date.
steps.expected:
Inventory movement is frozen (or warned) for the duration of
the count. Count sheets are generated covering every part.

steps.action:
Enter physical counts for each part. Some match system, some
have variance.
steps.expected: All counts capture. Variances are highlighted.

steps.action: Approve and post adjustments.
steps.expected:
System on-hand updates to match physical. GL postings reflect
shrinkage (variance value to a variance account).

steps.action: Movement freeze lifts.
steps.expected: Normal operations resume.

expected_overall: Physical inventory reconciles, variances post, freeze releases.
pass_criteria:
All parts counted AND variances posted AND GL shrinkage account
reflects the variance value.

negative_variants.title: Movement freeze blocks shipments and receipts
negative_variants.action: During the freeze, attempt a shipment and a vendor receipt.
negative_variants.expected:
Both are blocked with a clear "physical count in progress"
message until the freeze lifts.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No movement transactions complete during the freeze.

negative_variants.title: Cannot post adjustments without counts for every part
negative_variants.action: Try to approve adjustments while leaving some parts uncounted.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a clear list of parts still
requiring counts.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Posting refused until coverage is complete.

title: Issue the first purchase order
goal: Create the first real PO against a vendor and submit it.
preconditions: At least one vendor exists.
At least one part with vendor pricing exists.

steps.action: Find and open the purchase orders area. Choose to create a new PO.
steps.expected: The PO creation form opens. Required fields are clear.

steps.action:
Select Pacific Steel Supply. Add a line for 500 ft of
RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Verify default cost ($4.50/ft) and lead time
(14 days) populated automatically from vendor pricing.
steps.expected: Defaults populate correctly. Total amount displays.

steps.action:
Add a second line: 100 each of "Hardware-Bolt-M8" at a plausible
cost. Set ship-to: primary location.
steps.expected: Both lines visible. Order subtotal, tax, and total are calculated.

steps.action: Submit the PO.
steps.expected:
PO transitions to "Issued" or "Sent." It carries a stable PO
number. Status is visible to the vendor (if vendor portal exists)
or via printable PDF.

expected_overall:
First PO issued, with multiple lines, correct defaults, and
available downstream for receipt.
pass_criteria:
PO issued with stable number AND lines reflect entered values
AND it appears in the open-PO list.

negative_variants.title: Reject PO line with negative or zero quantity
negative_variants.action:
Try to add a PO line with quantity = -10, then again with
quantity = 0.
negative_variants.expected:
Both submissions are blocked with clear errors. PO does not
issue.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Both rejected AND error messages plain.

negative_variants.title: Reject PO to deactivated vendor
negative_variants.action:
Deactivate the vendor (per P2-VENDOR-005) and then try to issue
a PO referencing them.
negative_variants.expected:
Vendor is filtered out of the picker, OR submission is blocked
with a clear "vendor inactive" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No PO issued to inactive vendor.

title: PO with both inventory and fixed-asset lines
goal:
Verify a single PO can carry both inventory parts (consumed/sold)
and fixed-asset items (capitalized) — and that the GL impact at
receipt is correct for each line type.
preconditions: At least one vendor exists.
At least one fixed-asset GL account is present in the chart of accounts, available to assign on a fixed-asset PO line. (Established in P1-GL-001.)

steps.action:
Create a new PO to a vendor that supplies both. Add:
- Line 1: Inventory — 100 ft of RM-STEEL-1018-3X3
- Line 2: Fixed asset — 1 each of "Hydraulic Press 60-ton" at
  $35,000 (asset class, capitalize)
steps.expected:
Form differentiates the two. Fixed-asset line requires asset
class, capitalization GL account, and an estimated useful life.

steps.action: Submit the PO.
steps.expected: PO issued. Both line types visible.

expected_overall:
PO supports mixed line types. Distinction persists for downstream
receipt processing.
pass_criteria:
Both lines submitted on one PO AND each line carries its own
account/class designation.
why_this_matters:
ERPs that don't support mixed POs force buyers to issue separate
POs to the same vendor for the same delivery — that's friction
that real shops don't accept.

negative_variants.title: Fixed-asset line cannot post to inventory account
negative_variants.action:
Try to set a fixed-asset line's GL account to an inventory
account.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "fixed-asset line must post to
a capitalization account" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Account-class mismatch refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject fixed-asset line without estimated useful life
negative_variants.action: Try to save a fixed-asset line without entering useful life.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "useful life is required for
capitalization" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Useful life is mandatory on fixed-asset lines.

title: Amend a PO after issuance, before receipt
goal:
Verify POs can be revised (quantity change, price update) and the
vendor is notified, with a clear amendment trail.
preconditions: A purchase order has been issued to a vendor, has a stable PO number, and has at least one line for a part with quantity and price. (Established by P3-PO-001.)

steps.action: Open the issued PO. Use the amend or revise action.
steps.expected:
The PO becomes editable in revision mode. The original is
preserved.

steps.action: Increase line 1 quantity from 500 to 750 ft. Save.
steps.expected:
Amendment saves. PO version increments. Revision history
visible.

steps.action: Re-send to vendor (if there's a "send" action).
steps.expected:
Vendor receives the amended PO; original is superseded but
preserved for audit.

expected_overall: PO amendments are tracked. Vendors can see what changed.
pass_criteria:
Old version preserved AND new version active AND change history
visible.

negative_variants.title: Cannot reduce PO line below quantity already received
negative_variants.action:
After 100 of 500 ft has been received, try to amend the line
down to 50 ft total.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "amount already received exceeds
new quantity" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Amendment below received quantity is refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject amendment with no change reason
negative_variants.action:
Try to save the amendment without entering a reason / change
note.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns that amendments require a recorded
reason for audit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reason-less amendment refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot amend a fully closed PO
negative_variants.action: Close the PO (or fully receive it) and try to amend.
negative_variants.expected:
Amendment is unavailable on closed POs; the user must reopen
with explicit authority.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Closed-PO amendment refused.

title: Short-close a PO with remaining quantity unfilled
goal:
Verify that when a vendor cannot deliver the balance of a PO, the
buyer can short-close the PO so the line stops appearing on
on-order, GR/IR clears properly, and any remaining commitment is
released.
preconditions: A PO has a partially received line (e.g., 60 of 100 received).

steps.action:
Open the partially received PO. Use the short-close action with
a reason ("vendor cannot supply").
steps.expected:
Short-close is accepted. PO transitions to closed. Remaining
40 are no longer on-order.

steps.action: Run the open-PO list.
steps.expected: The PO is no longer present.

steps.action: Run the GR/IR aging.
steps.expected:
Only the actually received 60 are on GR/IR (or its equivalent).
The unfilled 40 do not leave a phantom GR/IR balance.

expected_overall: Short-close cleanly removes commitment without leaving GL phantoms.
pass_criteria:
PO closed AND open-PO list cleared AND GR/IR reflects only what
was received.

negative_variants.title: Short-close requires a reason
negative_variants.action: Try to short-close without entering a reason.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "reason is required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Short-close without reason refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot short-close after vendor invoice booked for full quantity
negative_variants.action:
Book a vendor invoice for the full ordered quantity, then try
to short-close.
negative_variants.expected:
Short-close is blocked with a clear "invoice booked for the
full amount; reverse the invoice first" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Short-close gated by invoiced quantity.

title: Receive against a PO at the dock
goal:
Record a vendor receipt — material physically arrived. Inventory
comes into the system; the PO line moves to received status.
preconditions: A purchase order has been issued to a vendor, has a stable PO number, and has at least one line for a part with quantity and price. (Established by P3-PO-001.)

steps.action:
Find and open the receiving area. Either scan the PO barcode
or search by PO number.
steps.expected:
The PO opens. Receivable lines are visible with their
ordered/received/remaining quantities.

steps.action:
Receive line 1: 500 ft of RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Enter lot number
"PSS-2026-04-001" (since the part is lot-tracked from P2-PART-003).
steps.expected:
Lot prompt appears (because the part is lot-tracked). Lot is
recorded.

steps.action: Receive line 2: 100 each of Hardware-Bolt-M8.
steps.expected: No lot prompt for non-lot-tracked part. Quantity received.

steps.action: Confirm and post the receipt.
steps.expected:
Inventory increases. PO status changes to "Received" or
"Closed." A receipt document is created and printable.

expected_overall:
First inventory exists. PO is closed. GL impact: inventory account
debited, GR/IR (goods received / invoice received) account credited.
pass_criteria:
Inventory increased by received quantity AND lot recorded for
lot-tracked items AND PO marked received.

negative_variants.title: Try to receive more than ordered
negative_variants.action:
On the same PO, try to enter receipt quantity 600 (greater
than 500 ordered).
negative_variants.expected:
The system warns or blocks the over-receipt. If allowed (with
tolerance), it must be flagged for review.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-receipt was either blocked OR flagged AND not silently accepted.

negative_variants.title: Try to receive without lot number on lot-tracked part
negative_variants.action: Try to receive line 1 without entering a lot number.
negative_variants.expected: The system blocks submission and explains that a lot is required.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Submission blocked AND error names the lot field.

title: Receive a partial shipment
goal:
Verify the system correctly handles a partial receipt — line stays
open, PO stays open, vendor expected to ship the balance.
preconditions: An issued PO exists with at least one line.

steps.action:
Open a PO with an unreceived line for 100 each of a part.
Receive only 60 of them.
steps.expected: Line accepts partial receipt. Remaining quantity (40) is visible.

steps.action: Save / post the receipt.
steps.expected:
Inventory increased by 60. PO line shows 60 received, 40 on order.
PO is still open.

expected_overall:
Partial receipts are supported. PO stays open until fully received
or short-closed.
pass_criteria:
Inventory matches partial quantity AND PO line still open AND
remaining quantity correct.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative or zero receipt quantity
negative_variants.action: Try to record a receipt of 0 or -10 units.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "quantity must be positive"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Non-positive receipt refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject receipt against a cancelled PO
negative_variants.action: Cancel a PO, then try to receive against it.
negative_variants.expected: The receipt is blocked with a clear "PO is cancelled" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Receipt against cancelled PO refused.

title: Receive partially with quality rejection
goal:
Verify that received material can be split between accepted and
rejected at the dock, with the rejected quantity returned or
isolated for quality review.
preconditions: An issued PO with a line for 500 ft of steel.

steps.action:
At receipt, mark 50 ft as "damaged on arrival" while accepting
the remaining 450 ft.
steps.expected:
The system supports splitting the receipt — accepted vs.
rejected. Rejected goes to a quarantine bin / status, NOT into
regular inventory.

steps.action: Initiate a return-to-vendor for the rejected 50 ft.
steps.expected:
A return-to-vendor (RTV) document is created. The rejected
material is isolated until the RTV ships.

expected_overall:
Receipt-time quality issues are handled in-line, not as a
follow-up workflow.
pass_criteria:
Accepted quantity in regular inventory AND rejected quantity
isolated AND RTV document created.

negative_variants.title: Reject damage entry without reason or quantity detail
negative_variants.action: Mark units as damaged but leave the damage reason blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; quality rejection requires a recorded
reason.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Damage entry without reason refused.

negative_variants.title: Rejected material does not enter sellable inventory
negative_variants.action:
After splitting accepted vs. rejected, query stock-on-hand for
the part.
negative_variants.expected:
Sellable on-hand reflects only the accepted quantity. The
rejected portion appears under quarantine / non-nettable
status.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No rejected material is selectable for picks or shipments.

title: Over-receipt within tolerance is allowed; beyond requires override
preconditions: An open PO line exists for a part with a defined over-receipt tolerance configured (in Procurement settings or per-vendor).

steps.action: For a 100-unit PO line, receive 105 units (5% over).
steps.expected:
Receipt is allowed (within tolerance). The 5-unit overage is
noted on the receipt but not blocked.

steps.action: For another 100-unit PO line, receive 120 units (20% over).
steps.expected:
Receipt is blocked or requires Buyer / Controller override.
The reason is clearly stated.

steps.action: Override (per role allowed). Receive the full 120.
steps.expected: Receipt completes. The override is audit-logged with reason.

expected_overall: Over-receipt tolerance is enforced and overrides are auditable.
pass_criteria:
In-tolerance receipt allowed AND beyond-tolerance blocked or
audited override AND audit captures the override.

negative_variants.title: Floor Operator role cannot apply over-receipt override
negative_variants.action:
As a Floor Operator (or any non-procurement role), attempt the
override.
negative_variants.expected:
The override action is unavailable; only authorized roles can
apply it.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Unauthorized role cannot override.

negative_variants.title: Override requires a documented reason
negative_variants.action: Attempt to apply the override without entering a reason.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or warns; reason is required for audit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reason-less override refused.

title: Submit a purchase requisition
goal:
Verify users can request purchases without having direct PO authority.
Requisitions go through approval before becoming a PO.
preconditions: Phase 2 master data exists: at least one vendor, one customer, raw and finished parts, a BOM, a routing, and pricing — all with their supporting fields populated. (Phase 2 outcomes.)
At least one part and one vendor exist.

steps.action:
Find and open the requisitions area. Submit a new requisition:
- Part: RM-STEEL-1018-3X3
- Quantity: 500 ft
- Needed by: 2 weeks from today
- Justification: short text
steps.expected: Requisition saves in "Pending approval" status.

steps.action:
Sign in as a user with PO approval authority. Open the
requisitions queue.
steps.expected: The pending requisition is visible.

steps.action: Approve the requisition.
steps.expected:
Requisition status changes to "Approved." Either auto-creates
a PO or surfaces a "Convert to PO" action.

expected_overall: Requisition workflow exists end-to-end.
pass_criteria: Requisition was submitted AND approved AND linked to a downstream PO.

negative_variants.title: Submitter cannot self-approve own requisition
negative_variants.action:
As the same user who submitted the requisition, attempt to
approve it.
negative_variants.expected: The approve action is unavailable to the submitter.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Self-approval refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject requisition with needed-by in the past
negative_variants.action:
Submit a requisition with a needed-by date one week before
today.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; past-dated need is almost always a
data entry error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Past needed-by surfaces a clear warning before submission.

negative_variants.title: Reject requisition with no justification
negative_variants.action: Submit a requisition with the justification field empty.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "justification is required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty justification refused.

title: Reserve inventory against a confirmed sales order
goal:
Verify on-hand inventory can be reserved against a confirmed SO
so it cannot be allocated to another order, while still showing
on-hand for valuation purposes.
preconditions: At least one confirmed SO with an in-stock part exists.

steps.action:
Open the confirmed SO. Reserve / allocate inventory against the
SO line.
steps.expected:
Reservation succeeds. The inventory record shows on-hand
unchanged but available reduced by the reserved quantity.

steps.action: Try to allocate the same inventory to a different SO.
steps.expected: Allocation is blocked or warns that available is insufficient.

steps.action: Cancel the original SO's reservation.
steps.expected: Reservation releases. Available equals on-hand again.

expected_overall:
Reservation locks supply for committed demand without changing
total on-hand.
pass_criteria:
Available reduces on reservation AND second allocation blocked AND
release restores availability.

negative_variants.title: Reject reservation exceeding on-hand
negative_variants.action: Try to reserve 999 units when only 50 are on hand.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "insufficient on-hand to reserve"
message naming the available quantity.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-reservation refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot reserve from a cancelled SO
negative_variants.action: Cancel the SO, then try to create a reservation against it.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "SO is cancelled" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reservation against cancelled SO refused.

title: Issue an RFQ to multiple vendors and compare returned quotes
goal:
Verify the request-for-quote flow: send identical RFQ to N vendors,
capture each vendor's response (price, lead time, MOQ), and compare
side-by-side.
preconditions: At least three vendors exist.

steps.action:
Find the RFQ area. Create an RFQ for 1,000 ft of a steel
material. Send to three vendors.
steps.expected:
RFQ is dispatched (email, portal, or document). Each vendor
shows as "responded: not yet" until reply.

steps.action:
For each vendor, enter the returned quote: price per foot, lead
time, MOQ.
steps.expected: Each response saves under the RFQ.

steps.action: Open the comparison view.
steps.expected:
Three quotes are visible side-by-side with totals computed (price
× MOQ-or-RFQ-quantity, plus lead time).

steps.action: Award the RFQ to one vendor and convert to a PO.
steps.expected:
A draft PO is created with the awarded vendor's terms. The
other quotes are preserved as historical context.

expected_overall: RFQ-to-PO works end-to-end with quote comparison.
pass_criteria:
RFQ dispatched AND three responses captured AND comparison correct
AND PO derived from the awarded quote.

negative_variants.title: Cannot award an RFQ before responses are captured
negative_variants.action: Try to award the RFQ when no vendor has responded.
negative_variants.expected:
The award action is unavailable or warns; awarding requires at
least one captured response.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty award refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject vendor response after RFQ closing date
negative_variants.action:
Set the RFQ closing date in the past, then try to enter a new
vendor response.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; late responses require explicit
acknowledgement.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Late responses are gated.

title: Configure safety stock and reorder point on a part
goal:
Set per-part safety stock (the minimum we never want to drop
below) and reorder point (the on-hand level that triggers a reorder
recommendation). Verify both feed MRP correctly.
preconditions: At least one raw material part exists.

steps.action:
Open the raw material part RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Find the planning
/ replenishment settings. Set safety stock = 100 ft, reorder
point = 200 ft.
steps.expected: Settings save.

steps.action:
Issue or adjust on-hand to 180 ft (below reorder point but
above safety stock).
steps.expected: On-hand updates.

steps.action: Run MRP (or open the reorder recommendations report).
steps.expected:
A reorder is recommended for this part. The recommended
quantity at minimum brings on-hand back to or above reorder
point — and aims for replenishment to the documented "order up
to" target.

expected_overall: Safety stock and reorder point drive replenishment recommendations.
pass_criteria:
Reorder recommended AND quantity targets restoring on-hand to /
above reorder point.

title: Apply customer payment to the invoice
goal:
Receive payment from the customer and apply it to the open
invoice — closing out the cycle.
preconditions: A customer invoice has been posted from a shipped sales order, AR has increased accordingly, and the invoice has been sent to the customer. (Established by P4-INV-001.)

steps.action:
Find the cash receipts area. Enter:
- Customer: ACME Industrial
- Payment amount: full invoice total
- Method: check, ACH, or wire
- Check / reference number
steps.expected: Form accepts the values.

steps.action: Apply the payment to the open invoice from P4-INV-001.
steps.expected:
System matches the payment to the invoice. Invoice status
changes to "Paid."

steps.action: Verify GL impact: cash debited, AR credited.
steps.expected: GL postings are correct.

expected_overall:
Quote-to-cash cycle complete. Invoice paid. Cycle profitability
can now be analyzed.
pass_criteria:
Payment applied AND invoice marked paid AND GL postings correct
AND AR balance for customer reduced.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate check / reference number for same customer
negative_variants.action:
Apply a payment, then try to apply a second payment with the
same check number for the same customer.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; duplicate check numbers are typically
data-entry errors.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate reference refused or surfaces an explicit warning.

negative_variants.title: Reject payment to a different customer's invoice
negative_variants.action:
Try to apply ACME's payment against an invoice issued to
another customer.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "payer does not match invoice
customer" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Cross-customer application refused.

title: Import a lockbox file and apply payments across multiple invoices
goal:
Verify the application can ingest a lockbox-style payment file (or
batch cash entry) and apply each payment to the right customer /
invoice combination, including handling unmatched payments.
preconditions: At least 5 open customer invoices across multiple customers.
A lockbox-format file (or equivalent batch entry) is available.

steps.action:
Find the lockbox / batch cash area. Upload a file (or batch-
enter) covering 5 payments — 4 that match invoices, 1 that
doesn't.
steps.expected:
Preview shows 4 matched and 1 unmatched. Unmatched is held in
a suspense / unapplied account, not lost.

steps.action: Confirm the application.
steps.expected:
4 invoices marked paid. 1 unapplied payment shows as customer
credit or in a suspense account.

steps.action:
Manually resolve the unmatched payment (apply to a customer
manually).
steps.expected: Payment applies. Suspense clears.

expected_overall:
Lockbox processes apply matched payments and surface unmatched
ones for resolution.
pass_criteria: 4 applied AND 1 held in suspense AND manual resolution clears it.

title: Apply a partial payment
goal:
Verify partial payments are supported — invoice stays open with
reduced balance.
preconditions: An open invoice exists.

steps.action: Receive a partial payment (e.g., 50% of an invoice). Apply.
steps.expected: Invoice balance reduces. Invoice stays open with remainder.

steps.action:
Verify aging is preserved (if invoice was 25 days old, it's
still 25 days old, not reset to 0).
steps.expected: Aging is correct. Customer statement shows the partial.

expected_overall: Partial payments work. Aging is preserved.
pass_criteria: Balance reduced by payment AND invoice still open AND aging unchanged.

negative_variants.title: Reject partial payment exceeding invoice balance
negative_variants.action:
Try to apply a partial payment that exceeds the remaining
invoice balance.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or surfaces overpayment-handling options;
silent over-application is refused.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-application is gated.

title: Operator clocks in for a shift; labor on a WO posts within shift hours
goal:
Verify that an operator's shift clock-in / clock-out is recorded
independently of WO-level labor, and that the day's WO labor sums
cannot exceed total shift attendance.
preconditions: A Floor Operator user exists.

steps.action:
As Floor Operator, scan the shift clock-in barcode at start of
shift.
steps.expected: Clock-in recorded with timestamp.

steps.action:
Start a WO and accumulate labor on it. Stop. Start another WO,
accumulate, stop.
steps.expected: Two WO labor entries are recorded inside the shift.

steps.action: Scan clock-out at end of shift.
steps.expected: Clock-out recorded.

steps.action: Run the daily attendance / labor report for this operator.
steps.expected:
Total shift hours displayed AND sum of WO labor ≤ shift hours.
Indirect / unclocked time is shown as a reconciling line.

expected_overall: Shift attendance and WO labor reconcile cleanly.
pass_criteria:
Shift hours captured AND WO labor sums fit inside shift AND any
difference shows as indirect time.

title: Issue a customer credit memo for a billing dispute (no return)
goal:
Verify a credit memo can be issued against an invoice for reasons
other than a return — pricing dispute, billing error, goodwill
credit — with appropriate GL impact and audit trail.
preconditions: At least one posted customer invoice exists.

steps.action:
Find the credit memo area. Create a new credit memo against an
existing invoice for $500 with reason: "Billing error — duplicate
charge."
steps.expected: Credit memo saves.

steps.action: Post the credit memo. Apply against the invoice.
steps.expected: AR reduces. GL: revenue (or contra-revenue) debited, AR credited.

steps.action: Open the audit log for the credit memo.
steps.expected: Reason, actor, timestamp, and amount are captured.

expected_overall: Standalone credit memo posts cleanly with GL and audit.
pass_criteria: Memo posted AND AR reduced AND reason captured.

title: Run an AR collections / dunning workflow on overdue invoices
goal:
Verify the system supports a structured collections process:
identify invoices past due, send reminder communications at
documented thresholds (e.g., 15 / 30 / 60 days), and capture a
collections note per customer.
preconditions: At least one customer has at least one invoice 15+ days past due.

steps.action:
Find the AR collections / dunning area. Run a collections
worklist for invoices past due 15+ days.
steps.expected:
Worklist appears with overdue invoices, age buckets, and
contact info.

steps.action:
Send the configured reminder communication for one invoice
(email or letter).
steps.expected:
Communication is generated. The customer record / invoice
records the touch.

steps.action:
Add a collections note ("Customer says check is in mail, will
follow up in 5 days").
steps.expected: Note saves with timestamp and user.

steps.action: Re-run the worklist with a "next-action-due" filter.
steps.expected: The invoice moves to a follow-up date based on the note.

expected_overall: Dunning loop captures reminders and follow-ups consistently.
pass_criteria:
Worklist correct AND reminder generated AND note recorded AND
follow-up surfaces correctly.

title: Complete the operation and move to the next
goal:
Mark the operation done — produce the expected quantity of good
pieces, optionally record scrap, and trigger the next operation.
preconditions: A work-order operation is in progress: an operator scanned the WO and the start action, the operation transitioned from Released to In Progress, labor time is tracking, and the kanban card has moved accordingly. (Established by P4-WO-START.)

steps.action:
Scan the WO and the complete-operation action. Enter:
- Good quantity: 98 (out of target 100)
- Scrap quantity: 2
- Scrap reason: "Setup miscut" (or similar)
steps.expected:
System accepts. Operation transitions to "Complete." Next
operation in routing transitions to "Ready."

steps.action:
Verify the kanban card moves from "In Progress" to "Ready"
for the next work center.
steps.expected:
Card moved. The work center shown on the card is the next in
routing.

expected_overall:
Operation is complete with good and scrap recorded. Next operation
is ready.
pass_criteria:
Quantities recorded AND scrap reason captured AND next operation
ready.

negative_variants.title: Reject completion exceeding remaining target
negative_variants.action:
Try to record good quantity 200 on a WO with target 100 and
no prior completions.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; over-completion requires explicit
override.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-completion gated.

negative_variants.title: Reject scrap entry without reason
negative_variants.action: Try to record any scrap quantity with the reason field blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "scrap reason is required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Scrap without reason refused.

title: Complete the final operation and move WO to inventory
goal:
When the last routing operation completes, finished goods enter
available inventory. If the part is serial-tracked, serial numbers
are generated.
preconditions: All preceding operations on the WO have completed.
WO is on the final inspection or final operation.

steps.action:
Complete the final operation (Inspection). Enter good quantity:
98.
steps.expected:
WO is closed. Finished goods inventory increases by 98 units.
If part is serial-tracked, prompt for serial numbers.

steps.action:
Verify the GL impact: WIP credited, finished goods inventory
debited, with cost based on standard or actual depending on
costing model.
steps.expected: GL postings are correct.

expected_overall: Finished goods are on the shelf. WO is closed. WIP is cleared.
pass_criteria: FG inventory increased AND WIP cleared AND any serials recorded.

negative_variants.title: Cannot final-complete with serials skipped on serial-tracked part
negative_variants.action:
Skip serial entry on a serial-tracked finished good and try
to confirm completion.
negative_variants.expected:
Completion is blocked with a clear "serial numbers required"
message naming each missing unit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Final completion gated by serial entry.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate serial across this and prior runs
negative_variants.action:
Enter a serial number that already exists on a previously
shipped unit of the same part.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "serial already used" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate serial refused.

title: Onboard a new employee end-to-end
goal:
Hire a new floor operator: complete employment paperwork (W-4,
I-9, direct deposit, background check, drug test status), assign
pay info, location, and trigger the rest.
preconditions: At least one location and pay structure exist (P1).

steps.action:
Find the new-hire workflow. Start onboarding for a new
employee:
- Name, address, contact info
- Hire date: today
- Pay: hourly, $22/hr, non-exempt, eligible for OT
- Location: primary
- Department: Production
steps.expected: Onboarding form accepts all values.

steps.action:
Capture or upload required documents:
- W-4 (federal withholding)
- I-9 (work authorization)
- Direct deposit info
- Background check cleared
- Drug test cleared
steps.expected:
Each document is recorded with date, status, and an audit
trail. Required documents are gated — onboarding can't complete
without them.

steps.action: Capture digital signatures where required.
steps.expected: Signatures are captured and stored with the document.

expected_overall:
New employee record is complete. Required compliance documentation
is in place. Employee is ready for system access and first
assignment.
pass_criteria:
Employee record exists AND all required documents present with
signatures AND no compliance gates open.
why_this_matters:
Missing I-9 paperwork on day-1 is a real fineable offense. The
system needs to be the source of truth for this, not a folder of
scanned PDFs.

negative_variants.title: Cannot complete onboarding with I-9 unsigned
negative_variants.action: Skip the I-9 signature and try to mark onboarding complete.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "I-9 signature required" message
naming each missing compliance gate.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Onboarding completion gated by I-9 signoff.

negative_variants.title: Reject hire date before person's earliest valid work date
negative_variants.action:
Set the hire date to a date earlier than the I-9 work-
authorization start date.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or warns; hire cannot precede authorization.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Pre-authorization hire date refused.

title: Grant system access to the new hire
goal:
Create a system user for the new employee, link them, and assign
the appropriate role.
preconditions: A new employee record exists with all required onboarding documents (W-4, I-9, direct deposit, background check, drug test) captured and any required digital signatures attached. (Established by P4-HIRE-001.)

steps.action: Open the new employee record. Initiate user creation.
steps.expected: A new user is created or invited via email.

steps.action: Assign the Floor Operator role.
steps.expected:
Role is assigned. User has the right permissions for floor
work.

steps.action:
Have the new hire sign in. Verify they see the floor app and
not administrative areas.
steps.expected: Role-appropriate UI is visible. Restricted areas are not.

expected_overall: New hire has system access. Their first sign-in shows the right UI.
pass_criteria:
User created AND linked to employee AND role assigned AND access
appropriately scoped.

negative_variants.title: Cannot grant access before onboarding compliance complete
negative_variants.action:
For an employee with incomplete I-9 / W-4, try to issue a
system user account.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a clear list of missing compliance
items.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Access provisioning gated by compliance.

title: Assign a work order operation to the new hire
goal:
Put the new hire on their first task — a real WO operation at a
work center where they're trained.
preconditions: The new employee has a system user account, the user is linked to the employee record, and a Floor Operator role is assigned. (Established by P4-HIRE-002.)
At least one open WO operation exists.

steps.action:
As a planner, open the kanban board. Drag (or assign) a
released operation to the new hire.
steps.expected: Assignment saves. The card is now tagged to the new hire.

steps.action: Sign in as the new hire. Open their assigned work list.
steps.expected: The assignment is visible. They can scan the WO and start work.

steps.action:
Verify their training records (if tracked) match the work
center / operation. If not trained, the system should warn.
steps.expected:
Either: they're trained and start; or: a training warning is
surfaced.

expected_overall: New hire is on their first task, with training validation if applicable.
pass_criteria:
Assignment visible to the operator AND any required training is
validated or flagged.

negative_variants.title: Cannot assign operation requiring missing certification
negative_variants.action:
Assign the new hire to a work center that requires a forklift
cert they do not have on file.
negative_variants.expected:
Assignment is blocked or warns; certification gating must be
explicit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Uncertified assignment refused or surfaces an explicit warning.

title: Generate the customer invoice
goal:
Bill the customer for the shipped goods, with correct line items,
taxes, and terms.
preconditions: The order has been handed off to a carrier, a tracking number is recorded on the SO, and the customer invoice has been triggered or queued. (Established by P4-SHIP.)

steps.action:
Open the AR / invoicing queue. The shipped SO appears as
ready to invoice.
steps.expected:
Invoice draft pre-populated with SO data: lines, prices, tax,
terms, ship-to.

steps.action:
Review the draft. Confirm the customer PO number is on the
invoice. Verify tax calculation matches expectations for the
ship-to jurisdiction.
steps.expected: Tax is calculated correctly based on customer's tax code.

steps.action: Post the invoice.
steps.expected:
AR balance for ACME increases. GL impact: AR debited, revenue
credited, sales tax payable credited.

steps.action: Send the invoice to the customer (email, PDF, EDI).
steps.expected: Invoice is delivered. Status changes to "Sent."

expected_overall: Customer invoice is in the customer's hands and on the AR ledger.
pass_criteria:
Invoice posted AND AR increased AND invoice sent AND GL postings
correct.

negative_variants.title: Reject invoice with no ship-to address resolved
negative_variants.action:
Clear or break the ship-to on the SO (e.g., the address is
flagged invalid). Try to generate the invoice.
negative_variants.expected:
Invoice generation is blocked with a clear "ship-to required for
taxation" message. Or, if a default ship-to fallback is used,
the fallback is named explicitly to the user — not silent.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Missing ship-to is surfaced AND invoice does not silently use a
wrong default.

negative_variants.title: Reject invoice in a closed period
negative_variants.action:
Close the period, then try to post an invoice dated in that
closed period.
negative_variants.expected: Posting is blocked with a clear "period closed" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Posting blocked AND message is plain.

title: Record labor time against the operation
goal:
Record actual labor time against the work order operation, so
labor cost variance can be calculated against the routing's
standard.
preconditions: A work-order operation is in progress: an operator scanned the WO and the start action, the operation transitioned from Released to In Progress, labor time is tracking, and the kanban card has moved accordingly. (Established by P4-WO-START.)

steps.action:
At the operation's completion (or at break / end of shift), use
the scanner or manual entry to clock out of this operation.
steps.expected:
Labor entry closes with start and end timestamps. Total time
is calculated.

steps.action:
Verify the labor cost is calculated using the work center's
default labor rate (from P1-WC-001).
steps.expected: Labor cost shows the rate × time. WO actual labor cost increases.

expected_overall: Labor time and cost are tracked against the WO and operation.
pass_criteria:
Labor entry closed AND cost calculated AND WO labor cost increased
by the entry.

negative_variants.title: Reject overlapping labor entries for same operator
negative_variants.action:
Try to clock the same operator into a second operation while
they are still clocked in to the first.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or surfaces a clear "operator already clocked
in" prompt requiring explicit reassignment.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Operator cannot be in two operations simultaneously.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative or zero labor duration
negative_variants.action:
Manually edit a closed labor entry so the end time precedes
the start.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "end must be after start" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Inverted labor times refused.

title: Issue material from inventory to the work order
goal:
Consume the BOM components from inventory and post them against the
WO so material variance can be tracked.
preconditions: A work-order operation is in progress: an operator scanned the WO and the start action, the operation transitioned from Released to In Progress, labor time is tracking, and the kanban card has moved accordingly. (Established by P4-WO-START.)
Inventory exists for the BOM components (from P3 receipt).

steps.action:
At the floor station, scan the WO. Choose to issue material.
The system shows the BOM and required quantities.
steps.expected:
For each BOM line, expected quantity is shown. Where lots are
required, a lot prompt appears.

steps.action:
Issue the steel: scan the lot "PSS-2026-04-001" and confirm
the required quantity (50 ft for 100 units).
steps.expected:
Lot is recorded against the WO. Inventory decreases by the
issued quantity.

steps.action: Issue the bolts and washers (no lots required).
steps.expected: Inventory decreases. WO material consumption is updated.

expected_overall:
Material is consumed from inventory and traced against the WO,
including lot traceability for lot-tracked parts.
pass_criteria:
Inventory decreased correctly AND lot trace recorded AND WO shows
material issued matches BOM.

negative_variants.title: Insufficient inventory to issue
negative_variants.action: Try to issue a quantity greater than what's available in stock.
negative_variants.expected:
System blocks the issue or warns. Either offers an alternate
lot, requires a backorder, or refuses to consume more than
on-hand.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Issue blocked or warned AND inventory not over-issued.

title: Apply an overpayment as customer credit
goal:
Handle when a customer pays more than the invoice — the difference
becomes credit on file, applicable to future invoices.
preconditions: An open invoice exists.

steps.action: Receive a payment greater than the invoice total. Apply.
steps.expected: Invoice closes. Excess shows as customer credit / unapplied cash.

steps.action: Open the customer record.
steps.expected: Available credit is visible.

expected_overall:
Overpayments produce customer credit, not refunds (unless
explicitly chosen).
pass_criteria: Invoice closed AND credit recorded AND visible on customer record.

negative_variants.title: Cannot refund overpayment without controller approval
negative_variants.action:
As a Sales user, try to convert the customer credit to a
cash refund.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is unavailable to the role; refunds require
controller approval.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Self-service refund refused for non-authorized roles.

title: Pack the order with proper documentation
goal:
Pack picked goods into shipping containers with packing slip and
any customer-specific labeling requirements.
preconditions: All sales-order lines have been picked at the bin, allocated to the SO, and staged for shipping. (Established by P4-PICK.)

steps.action:
Open the pack area. Pull up the staged SO. Scan items into a
shipping container.
steps.expected:
Each item is verified against the SO line. Mismatches are
flagged.

steps.action: Generate a packing slip and any required labels.
steps.expected:
Documents print or download. They include SO number, customer
PO, line items, and any special instructions.

expected_overall: Order is packed and documented, ready to hand off to the carrier.
pass_criteria: Packing complete AND packing slip generated AND items match the SO.

negative_variants.title: Reject pack scan of item not on the SO
negative_variants.action: Scan a part not present on the SO into the shipping container.
negative_variants.expected: The scan is rejected with a clear "item not on order" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Out-of-order item refused at pack.

negative_variants.title: Reject completion of packing with missing items
negative_variants.action:
Try to mark the order packed while one SO line still has items
unscanned.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a list of remaining lines.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Pack incomplete refused until short-ship is documented.

title: Pick inventory for the sales order
goal:
Allocate finished goods to the SO and pick them physically — the
start of the shipping side of the cycle.
preconditions: Finished goods have been moved from the staging area to a storage bin and are visible in the available-to-pick view at the new location. (Established by P4-PUTAWAY.)
Finished goods are available.

steps.action: Open the pick queue or the SO. Generate a pick list.
steps.expected:
Pick list shows: SO number, customer, items, quantities, bin
locations. If serials are required, the list includes serials
to scan during pick.

steps.action:
Pick at the bin: scan the part, confirm quantity (and serials
if applicable).
steps.expected: Each pick scan reduces bin quantity. Pick list updates.

steps.action: Mark the pick complete.
steps.expected:
Goods are staged for shipping. SO line is allocated and ready
to ship.

expected_overall: Pick is complete. Goods are at the staging area, ready to ship.
pass_criteria:
All pick lines complete AND inventory location updated AND SO
allocated.

negative_variants.title: Reject pick of wrong serial when serials are required
negative_variants.action: Scan a serial number not on the SO's allocation list.
negative_variants.expected:
Pick is blocked with a clear "this serial is not allocated to
the order" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Wrong serial refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject pick exceeding allocated quantity
negative_variants.action: Try to pick 110 when the SO line allocates 100.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or warns; over-pick must be explicit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-pick gated.

title: Move finished goods from the staging area to a bin
goal:
After WO completion, physically move the goods to a storage bin
and record the move.
preconditions: The final routing operation is complete: WO is closed, finished goods are in inventory, WIP is cleared, and any required serial numbers have been recorded. (Established by P4-COMP-FINAL.)

steps.action:
In the put-away area, scan the part / WO label. Scan or select
the destination bin (e.g., "FG-BIN-A1").
steps.expected: System records the move. Inventory location updates.

steps.action: Verify the part is now allocatable from that bin.
steps.expected: Available-to-pick view shows the goods in the new bin.

expected_overall:
Finished goods are at their storage location and available for
pick.
pass_criteria: Goods at correct bin AND available for downstream allocation.

negative_variants.title: Reject put-away to bin restricted to a different part
negative_variants.action:
Try to put away FG-BRACKET-A1 to a bin restricted to raw
material storage.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "bin restriction" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Restricted bin assignment refused.

title: Create a quote for a customer
goal:
Build a quote for a customer — pricing, lead time, terms — that
can be accepted and converted to a sales order.
preconditions: At least one customer (P2-CUST-001).
At least one finished part with pricing (P2-PART-002, P2-PRICE-001).

steps.action: Find and open the quotes area. Choose to create a new quote.
steps.expected: Quote creation form opens.

steps.action:
Select ACME Industrial as the customer. Add a line: 100 each of
FG-BRACKET-A1.
steps.expected:
Default unit price is $35.00 (Distributor Pricing list from
P2-PRICE-001), not the part's $45 list.

steps.action:
Verify lead time defaults from the part's master data. Add a
validity period (e.g., quote good for 30 days). Save.
steps.expected:
Quote saves with a stable quote number, all calculated totals
visible, and validity dates set.

steps.action:
Send the quote to the customer (email, PDF download, or whatever
the system supports).
steps.expected: Quote is delivered. Status changes to "Sent."

expected_overall:
First quote exists, with correct customer pricing applied, and is
in the customer's hands.
pass_criteria: Quote saved AND priced from the right list AND sent to the customer.

negative_variants.title: Reject zero-quantity line on a quote
negative_variants.action: Try to add a quote line with quantity = 0.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or the line is removed automatically with a
clear message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Zero-quantity line not silently saved.

negative_variants.title: Reject quote referencing a deactivated customer
negative_variants.action:
Deactivate ACME Industrial. Try to create a quote referencing
them.
negative_variants.expected:
Customer is filtered out, or selection is blocked with a clear
"customer inactive" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No quote created against inactive customer.

title: Override default pricing on a quote line
goal:
Verify a salesperson can override default pricing for a one-off
deal, with the override flagged for visibility.
preconditions: A quote has been created for a customer with the right customer-list pricing applied, validity dates set, and the quote sent. (Established by P4-QUOTE-001.)

steps.action:
Create a new quote for ACME with a line for 200 units of
FG-BRACKET-A1. Override the unit price from $35 to $30 (deeper
discount).
steps.expected:
Override is accepted with a clear visual indicator showing
the price was overridden.

steps.action: Save and view the quote.
steps.expected:
Override is visible. Margin or markup analysis (if available)
shows the impact.

expected_overall: Pricing overrides are supported and surfaced clearly.
pass_criteria:
Override visible AND price reflected on the quote AND override
flag visible to reviewers.

negative_variants.title: Override below cost requires controller approval
negative_variants.action: Override the price to a value below the part's standard cost.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or routes for controller approval. The
below-cost condition is named explicitly.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Below-cost override is gated.

negative_variants.title: Reject override with no documented reason
negative_variants.action: Override the price without entering an override reason.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or warns that overrides require a reason.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reason-less override refused.

title: Convert an accepted quote to a sales order
goal:
Move from quote to sales order — the customer agreed, now we
commit production and delivery.
preconditions: A quote has been created for a customer with the right customer-list pricing applied, validity dates set, and the quote sent. (Established by P4-QUOTE-001.)

steps.action: Open the quote. Use the convert-to-SO action.
steps.expected:
A new sales order is created with all quote lines, prices, and
terms carried over.

steps.action:
Add or confirm the customer PO number, requested ship date,
and ship-to address.
steps.expected: All values save.

steps.action: Submit / confirm the SO.
steps.expected:
SO transitions to "Open" or "Booked." Quote is marked as
converted.

expected_overall: SO exists and references the original quote for traceability.
pass_criteria:
SO is open AND links to the original quote AND carries all the
quote's pricing.

negative_variants.title: Cannot convert an expired quote
negative_variants.action:
Set the quote's validity to a date in the past, then try to
convert.
negative_variants.expected:
The convert action is blocked or warns; expired quotes require
explicit re-issue or extension.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Expired quote conversion is gated.

negative_variants.title: Cannot convert the same quote twice
negative_variants.action: Convert the quote, then attempt to convert it again.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is unavailable or surfaces a clear "already
converted" message linking to the SO.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Double conversion refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject SO without a customer PO number when required
negative_variants.action:
Confirm the SO without entering the customer's PO number on a
customer that requires PO references.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; PO reference is enforced per
customer setting.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Missing required PO is surfaced before SO confirmation.

title: Revise a sent quote and preserve the prior version
goal:
Verify a quote that has been sent to a customer can be revised
without losing the original — both versions are preserved, the
customer-facing copy clearly shows it's revision 2, and prior
revisions remain auditable.
preconditions: At least one sent quote exists.

steps.action: Open a sent quote. Use the revise / amend action.
steps.expected:
A new revision (e.g., Rev B) is staged. The original is
preserved.

steps.action: Change one line price. Add a new line. Save and re-send.
steps.expected:
Customer-facing document shows Rev B clearly. Quote number
stays the same; revision letter / number increments.

steps.action: Open the quote history.
steps.expected:
Both revisions visible. The customer can be told which revision
they should look at.

expected_overall:
Quote revisions preserve prior versions and clearly mark the active
one.
pass_criteria: Both revisions visible AND active rev marked AND prior rev is read-only.

title: A quote past its validity date cannot be converted without re-acceptance
goal:
Verify that a quote whose validity date has passed cannot be
converted to an SO without explicit re-acceptance / re-issue, and
is visually flagged as expired in the quote list.
preconditions: At least one quote with a validity period exists.

steps.action:
Find or create a quote with validity ending yesterday (or
backdate the quote so its window has lapsed).
steps.expected: Quote saves.

steps.action: Open the quote list.
steps.expected:
The expired quote is visibly marked (e.g., "Expired" badge or
strikethrough date).

steps.action: Try to convert the expired quote to an SO.
steps.expected:
Conversion is blocked or warned, with an option to re-issue
the quote (extend validity) before converting.

steps.action: Re-issue the quote with a new validity. Convert to SO.
steps.expected: New conversion succeeds.

expected_overall: Expiration is enforced; re-issue is the proper path forward.
pass_criteria:
Expired conversion blocked AND re-issue path works AND list flags
expired status.

title: Ship the order
goal:
Hand off the packed order to a carrier — domestic or international —
capturing tracking information and triggering the customer invoice.
preconditions: The order is packed into shipping containers, the packing slip has been generated, and items have been verified against the SO. (Established by P4-PACK.)
A shipping integration is configured (P0-INTEG-002) — or manual shipment entry.

steps.action:
Open the ship area. Select the packed order. Choose a carrier
and service level (or use the default).
steps.expected:
System produces a shipping label and tracking number — either
via carrier integration or manual entry.

steps.action: Confirm the ship.
steps.expected:
SO marks as shipped. Tracking number is recorded on the SO.
Inventory clears the staging area. The customer is (optionally)
notified.

steps.action:
Verify the next step: an invoice is either auto-generated or
surfaces in the AR queue.
steps.expected: Invoice is created from the shipped SO.

expected_overall:
Shipment is on its way. SO is shipped. Customer invoice is queued
or created.
pass_criteria: Shipment created AND tracking number recorded AND invoice triggered.

negative_variants.title: Reject ship without packing complete
negative_variants.action: Try to ship before the order has been packed.
negative_variants.expected:
The ship action is unavailable or blocked with a "pack first"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Ship-before-pack refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject ship to address with no postal validation
negative_variants.action:
Try to ship to an address missing postal code or with a
malformed one.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; the carrier cannot accept the
shipment without a valid postal code.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Bad ship-to address surfaces a clear validation error.

title: Ship internationally with customs documentation
goal:
Verify international shipments produce the right customs paperwork
(commercial invoice, harmonized codes, country-of-origin).
preconditions: At least one international customer (P2-CUST-004).
That customer has an SO ready to ship.

steps.action:
In the ship workflow for the Munich Manufacturing GmbH order,
verify the customs documentation prompts appear.
steps.expected:
Form requests harmonized tariff codes, country of origin,
commercial-invoice value, and incoterm.

steps.action: Fill in the required fields and confirm.
steps.expected:
Commercial invoice and any other required documents (e.g.,
certificate of origin) are generated.

expected_overall: International shipments produce complete customs paperwork.
pass_criteria:
Commercial invoice generated AND harmonized codes captured AND
incoterm recorded.

negative_variants.title: Reject international shipment without harmonized code
negative_variants.action:
Try to confirm the shipment leaving the harmonized tariff code
blank on at least one line.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear list of lines missing the code.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Missing tariff codes refused at ship.

negative_variants.title: Reject incoterm not in the supported list
negative_variants.action:
Try to free-text an incoterm value (e.g., "DDPish") rather
than picking from the controlled list.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or restricts input to the supported set
(EXW, FOB, DAP, DDP, etc.).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Invalid incoterm refused.

title: An SO ships in two partial shipments, each with its own invoice
goal:
Verify a sales order can ship in multiple deliveries, each with
its own packing slip and invoice, with the SO remaining open until
fully shipped.
preconditions: A confirmed SO with a quantity that can be split exists.
Inventory is partially available (e.g., 60 of 100 on hand).

steps.action:
Pick, pack, and ship 60 of the 100 ordered units. Generate
shipment 1.
steps.expected:
First packing slip and tracking are produced. SO remains open
with 40 units on backorder.

steps.action: Generate the partial invoice for the 60 shipped.
steps.expected: Invoice 1 posts for the 60 units. AR increases.

steps.action:
Once 40 more units become available, pick / pack / ship them
as shipment 2.
steps.expected: Second packing slip generated. SO closes after this shipment.

steps.action: Generate invoice 2 for the remaining 40.
steps.expected: Invoice 2 posts. SO is fully invoiced.

expected_overall:
Split shipments produce separate invoices and close the SO when
fully fulfilled.
pass_criteria:
Two shipments AND two invoices AND SO closes only after second
shipment.

title: Release a work order from a sales order
goal:
Generate and release a work order to fulfill the SO. The WO carries
the BOM and routing from the part's master data and is assigned to
the appropriate work centers.
preconditions: A sales order has been created from an accepted quote, in Open or Booked status, with customer PO number, requested ship date, and ship-to address all confirmed. (Established by P4-QUOTE-003.)
The part on the SO has a BOM and routing.

steps.action:
Open the sales order. Use the "release to production" or
"create work order" action.
steps.expected:
Work order is generated. WO number, target quantity, BOM
revision, and routing are all populated.

steps.action:
Verify the operations match the part's routing — Cut, Weld,
Paint, Inspect at the right work centers.
steps.expected:
All operations are present in the right order with the work
centers from P2-ROUTE-001.

steps.action: Release the WO to production.
steps.expected:
WO status changes from "Planned" to "Released" (or equivalent).
It now appears on the floor's work-to-do list.

expected_overall:
Work order is on the floor, with BOM and routing locked at the
revision active when released.
pass_criteria:
WO released AND links to its source SO AND its BOM/routing
revisions match what was active at release.

negative_variants.title: Warn on insufficient material at release
negative_variants.action:
Reduce on-hand of a BOM component below what the WO would need.
Attempt to release the WO.
negative_variants.expected:
Application warns about projected material shortage, and either
blocks release until material is on order, or releases with a
clear "shortage" flag visible to the floor and planner.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Shortage was surfaced AND release outcome is explicit (blocked
or flagged), not silent.

negative_variants.title: Reject release without an active routing
negative_variants.action:
Mark the part's routing as draft / inactive (or pick a part
without a routing). Try to release.
negative_variants.expected: Release is blocked with a clear "no active routing" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Release blocked AND error names missing routing.

title: Verify the work order appears on the kanban / sprint board
goal:
When a WO is released, it should show up as a card on the
kanban-style task board so the production team can see and pick
it up.
preconditions: A work order has been released from a sales order. The WO carries a stable number, references the BOM and routing active at release, and is in Released status — visible to the floor but not yet started. (Established by P4-WO-001.)

steps.action: Open the kanban / task board for the production area.
steps.expected:
The newly released WO appears as a card in the appropriate
"Ready" or "Released" column.

steps.action: Open the card.
steps.expected:
Card shows: WO number, part, target quantity, due date, current
operation, work center.

expected_overall: Released WOs are surfaced as kanban cards for the floor to pick up.
pass_criteria:
WO is visible on the board AND card has identifying details AND
state matches the WO's released status.

negative_variants.title: Closed WOs do not appear on the active kanban board
negative_variants.action:
Close (or cancel) a WO. Open the kanban board for that
production area.
negative_variants.expected:
The closed WO is not shown on the active board; it appears
only in a "completed" or filtered view.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Active board does not show closed WOs.

title: A WO configured to backflush consumes BOM materials at completion
goal:
Verify that a part / WO marked for backflush automatically consumes
BOM-defined component quantities from inventory at completion time,
rather than requiring an explicit issue per operation.
preconditions: At least one finished part has its BOM configured for backflush.
A WO for that part is released and at completion.

steps.action: Note the inventory of the BOM components before completion.
steps.expected: Quantities visible.

steps.action:
Complete the final operation on the WO with good quantity, e.g.,
100 units.
steps.expected:
Completion accepts. The BOM components are automatically
consumed at the per-unit quantity × 100 — no explicit issue
step required.

steps.action: Verify component inventory dropped by the expected amount.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
For lot-tracked components, verify a lot was consumed (FEFO
default).
steps.expected: Lot trace recorded.

expected_overall: Backflush correctly consumes materials at completion.
pass_criteria:
Inventory dropped by exact BOM-extended quantity AND lot trace
recorded for lot-tracked components.

title: Start the first operation on a released work order
goal:
Begin work at the floor: scan or pick the WO, identify the work
center, and transition the operation from Released to In Progress.
preconditions: A work order has been released from a sales order. The WO carries a stable number, references the BOM and routing active at release, and is in Released status — visible to the floor but not yet started. (Established by P4-WO-001.)

steps.action:
At the work center's station (paired scanner workstation, or
mobile device). Sign in or scan badge. Scan the WO ID. Scan
the start barcode (or tap the start action).
steps.expected:
The combination of barcodes resolves: this WO, this work
center, this operator, start. Operation transitions to
"In Progress."

steps.action: Verify the kanban card has moved from "Ready" to "In Progress."
steps.expected:
The card moved automatically without anyone touching the kanban
UI.

steps.action:
Verify labor time is now being tracked against the WO and
operator.
steps.expected: A labor entry is open and accumulating against the operator.

expected_overall: Operation is in progress. Labor is tracking. Kanban reflects status.
pass_criteria:
Status changed AND operator recorded AND labor time accumulating
AND kanban moved.

negative_variants.title: Cannot start an operation already in progress on another operator
negative_variants.action:
As a second operator, scan the same operation that another
operator has already started.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or warns; the operation cannot have two
simultaneous owners (or requires explicit handoff).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate start is refused or routed through a handoff flow.

negative_variants.title: Cannot start an operation out of routing order
negative_variants.action:
Try to start the second routing operation while the first is
still released.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a clear "previous operation must
complete first" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Out-of-order start refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot start without scanning operator badge
negative_variants.action:
Try to scan the WO and start without first identifying the
operator.
negative_variants.expected: The start action is blocked until an operator is identified.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Anonymous start refused.

title: Review and explain WO variance before closing the work order
goal:
Verify that when a WO closes, the system surfaces material and
labor variance vs. standard, and the user can attach a variance
reason before the close commits.
preconditions: A WO has completed all operations and is ready to close.

steps.action: Open a completed WO ready to close. Open the variance summary.
steps.expected:
Material variance (actual issue cost - standard BOM cost),
labor variance (actual hours × rate - standard hours × rate),
and total cost variance are all shown.

steps.action:
For non-zero variance, attach a reason code (e.g., "Setup
scrap higher than standard").
steps.expected: Reason saves on the WO.

steps.action: Close the WO.
steps.expected:
WO closes. Variance posts to the appropriate variance accounts
(material variance, labor variance) per the costing model.

expected_overall: WO variance is surfaced, explained, and posted to GL.
pass_criteria: Variance correct AND reason captured AND GL postings tie.

title: Configure declining-balance depreciation on a fixed asset
goal:
Verify the application supports depreciation methods beyond
straight-line — specifically declining balance (e.g., 200% DB) —
and that period postings match the method's math.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset is being created or can have its method changed (typically only at creation).

steps.action:
Create a new asset with method = 200% declining balance,
useful life 5 years, cost $10,000.
steps.expected:
Asset saves. Schedule shows year-1 depreciation ≈ $4,000
(40% of $10,000), year-2 ≈ $2,400 (40% of $6,000 remaining),
etc.

steps.action: Run period depreciation for the first period.
steps.expected: Posting equals (year-1 depreciation / 12) for a monthly close.

steps.action: Verify GL posting is correct.
steps.expected:
Depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation post per the
method.

expected_overall: Declining-balance depreciation works correctly.
pass_criteria:
Schedule matches method math AND period posting equals expected
amount.
notes:
Many ERPs ship straight-line only. If declining balance is not
available, that's a real limitation — flag rather than soften.

title: Retire a fixed asset (dispose, scrap, sell)
goal:
Verify retirement of a fixed asset stops further depreciation,
posts the appropriate gain or loss on disposal, and removes the
asset from operational lists (PM scheduling, work order references).
preconditions: At least one fixed asset has been depreciating (P5-CLOSE-003).

steps.action:
Open the asset. Use the retire / dispose action. Choose method:
"Sold" with proceeds = $5,000.
steps.expected:
Retirement is staged. The application computes net book value
vs. proceeds and shows the resulting gain or loss.

steps.action: Confirm.
steps.expected:
Asset transitions to Retired. GL impact: cost and accumulated
depreciation cleared, cash debited for proceeds, and gain or
loss posted to the right account.

steps.action: Run period depreciation again.
steps.expected: The retired asset is excluded — no further depreciation posts.

steps.action: Try to schedule a PM against the retired asset.
steps.expected: Operation is blocked with a clear "asset retired" message.

expected_overall:
Retirement stops depreciation, posts disposal gain/loss, and gates
operational use.
pass_criteria:
Asset retired AND gain/loss posted AND no further depreciation AND
PM scheduling blocked.

title: Transfer a fixed asset to another location with audit trail
goal:
Verify a fixed asset can be transferred to a different location and
/ or work center, the transfer is recorded with date and reason,
and depreciation continues uninterrupted.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset exists at a known location (P1-ASSET-001).
At least two locations exist (P1-LOC-002).

steps.action:
Open the asset Press 1. Use the transfer / move action. Set
destination location and effective date.
steps.expected: Transfer is accepted. Asset's current location updates.

steps.action: Open the asset's history.
steps.expected:
Transfer event recorded with date, prior location, new location,
reason, and actor.

steps.action:
Run period depreciation. Verify the asset still depreciates on
its existing schedule (no reset).
steps.expected: Depreciation continues per the same schedule.

expected_overall: Asset transfer changes location without disrupting financials.
pass_criteria:
Location updated AND history captures the transfer AND depreciation
schedule unchanged.

title: Reconcile a bank account against a statement
goal:
Verify the bank reconciliation workflow: import or enter the
bank statement, match each line to a system record (deposit,
payment, JE), identify unmatched items, post adjustments where
appropriate, and close the reconciliation with a balanced result.
preconditions: A cash GL account exists.
At least one customer payment and one vendor payment have posted in the period.

steps.action:
Find the bank reconciliation area. Start a new reconciliation
for the cash account, with a statement ending balance and date.
steps.expected:
Reconciliation form opens with system register on one side,
statement entries on the other.

steps.action:
Import or paste the bank statement (or enter manually). Match
each statement line to a system record.
steps.expected: Matched items clear. Unmatched items remain in their column.

steps.action:
For an unmatched bank line (e.g., bank fee), post an adjusting
JE.
steps.expected: JE posts. The line clears the reconciliation.

steps.action: Close the reconciliation.
steps.expected:
The system confirms reconciled balance equals statement ending
balance. The reconciliation is saved.

expected_overall:
Bank reconciliation closes balanced, capturing matches and
adjustments.
pass_criteria:
Reconciled balance = statement balance AND adjustments posted AND
reconciliation saved.

title: Inventory reconciliation as part of period close
goal:
Run an inventory reconciliation report comparing system-on-hand
to GL inventory. Investigate any variance.
preconditions: Phase 4 has run at least one full quote-to-cash cycle: a work order has consumed material, finished goods went to inventory, an order was shipped, an invoice was posted, and cash was applied. (Phase 4 outcomes.)

steps.action:
Find the inventory reconciliation report. Run as of the last
day of the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows total inventory value (system) vs. GL inventory
account balance.
