steps.action:
Investigate any discrepancy. (If none — the case still passes;
that's the goal.)
steps.expected: Discrepancy explained or absent.

expected_overall: Inventory ties to GL. Any variance is identified and explainable.
pass_criteria: Reconciliation runs AND variance (if any) is explainable.

negative_variants.title: Reject reconciliation report run as of a future date
negative_variants.action: Try to run the reconciliation as of a date in the future.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or warns; the report only meaningfully
runs at past or current period end.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Future-dated reconciliation refused.

title: Take AR and AP aging snapshots at period close
goal:
Snapshot AR and AP aging at the period boundary so the historical
position is recoverable later.
preconditions: AR and AP both have transaction history: at least one customer invoice exists in AR (from Phase 4) and at least one vendor invoice and payment exist in AP (from Phase 3). (Phase 3 + Phase 4 outcomes.)

steps.action: Run AR aging report as of the last day of the period.
steps.expected:
Report displays buckets (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+) and
totals per customer.

steps.action: Run AP aging report similarly.
steps.expected: AP aging displays per vendor.

steps.action: Save / archive both reports for audit purposes.
steps.expected: Both reports are stored or downloadable.

expected_overall: Period-end aging is captured.
pass_criteria:
Both reports run AND saved AND totals tie to AR and AP control
accounts on the GL.

title: Run period depreciation on fixed assets
goal:
Calculate and post the period's depreciation across all fixed
assets.
preconditions: Fixed assets exist with depreciation schedules (P3-ASSET-COMM-001).

steps.action: Open the depreciation run area. Select the period.
steps.expected:
System calculates depreciation per asset based on each asset's
schedule.

steps.action: Review the calculation. Post.
steps.expected:
Depreciation entries post: depreciation expense debited,
accumulated depreciation credited, per asset and rolled up.

expected_overall: Period depreciation is posted. Fixed asset book values reduce.
pass_criteria:
Depreciation posted AND amounts match expected straight-line
calculations AND book values updated.

negative_variants.title: Cannot run depreciation twice for same period
negative_variants.action:
After posting depreciation for the period, attempt to run it
again for the same period.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or surfaces a clear "already run for
this period" warning.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Double-run depreciation refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject depreciation for an asset retired before the period
negative_variants.action:
Run depreciation for a period that begins after an asset's
retirement date.
negative_variants.expected:
The retired asset is excluded from the run; depreciation does
not continue past retirement.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Retired assets do not depreciate.

title: Lock the period and prevent further postings
goal:
Once close is complete, lock the period to prevent backdated entries
that would invalidate already-published reports.
preconditions: All period adjustments and depreciation have posted.

steps.action: Find the period close action. Confirm closing.
steps.expected:
A confirmation requests the period close. After confirmation,
the period is locked.

steps.action: Try to post a journal entry dated within the closed period.
steps.expected:
System blocks the entry. The error explains the period is
closed and offers either an override (with permission) or a
route to the next open period.

steps.action:
Verify financial reports for the closed period are now
"snapshot" — running them produces the same numbers always.
steps.expected: Reports are stable.

expected_overall: Period is closed. Reports are stable. Backdated postings are blocked.
pass_criteria: Period locked AND backdated post blocked AND reports stable.
why_this_matters:
Without period close, every "month-end report" can change next time
it's run because someone backdated an entry. That destroys
accountability.

negative_variants.title: Reopen a closed period as controller
negative_variants.action:
As controller (with override authority), reopen the closed
period.
negative_variants.expected:
Reopen requires confirmation and is logged in an audit trail
(who reopened, when, why).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reopen is gated AND audit-logged.

title: Close the fiscal year and roll income-statement balances to retained earnings
goal:
Verify year-end close: all periods closed, year-end roll posts
closing entries that zero income-statement accounts and post net
income to retained earnings, and the new fiscal year opens with
balance-sheet carryforward only.
preconditions: All periods of the fiscal year are closed.
Net income for the year is computed (P&L).

steps.action: Find the year-end close action. Trigger the year-end roll.
steps.expected:
Application generates closing entries: revenue and expense
accounts zero out, net amount posts to Retained Earnings.

steps.action: Confirm and post.
steps.expected: Closing entries post. New fiscal year is open.

steps.action: Run the new year's opening trial balance.
steps.expected:
Asset, liability, and equity balances carry forward. Revenue
and expense accounts open at zero.

steps.action: Try to post a transaction dated in the closed prior year.
steps.expected:
Blocked unless the user has explicit re-open authority (per
P5-CLOSE-004 pattern).

expected_overall: Year closes, P&L resets, balance sheet carries forward.
pass_criteria:
Closing entries post AND new year opens with zeroed P&L AND
carried-forward BS AND prior year is locked.

title: Run a routine cycle count
goal:
Perform a routine cycle count (e.g., ABC-classified parts) as part
of normal operations rather than year-end physical inventory.
preconditions: Inventory exists across multiple parts.

steps.action:
Generate a cycle count for parts due to be counted (system
should know which based on ABC class and last count).
steps.expected: Count list is generated automatically.

steps.action: Count physically. Enter results.
steps.expected: Quantities recorded. Variances flagged.

steps.action: Approve and post adjustments for variance.
steps.expected: Inventory adjusted. Last-count date updates per part.

expected_overall: Routine cycle count runs end-to-end. ABC schedule advances.
pass_criteria: Counts captured AND adjustments posted AND last-count dates updated.

title: Report damage to a piece of equipment
goal:
Floor operator notices damage on equipment (a press hydraulic leak,
a worn fixture) and logs it. A maintenance ticket is created.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset record (P1-ASSET-001).

steps.action:
At the press, scan the asset tag. Choose "Report damage" from
the asset's actions.
steps.expected: A damage report form opens, pre-filled with the asset.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Description: "Hydraulic fluid leak from main cylinder"
- Severity: Medium (operable but degraded)
- Photos (if supported): one or two
steps.expected: Form accepts. Severity drives downstream priority.

steps.action: Submit. Verify a maintenance ticket / work order is created.
steps.expected:
A maintenance WO appears in the maintenance queue, linked to
the asset.

expected_overall: Damage is logged, photographed, and dispatched to maintenance.
pass_criteria:
Maintenance WO created AND linked to the asset AND visible in the
maintenance queue.

negative_variants.title: Reject damage report on a retired asset
negative_variants.action:
Retire an asset (per P5-ASSET-RETIRE-001). Try to file a damage
report against it.
negative_variants.expected: Report is blocked with a clear "asset is retired" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No report created against retired asset.

title: Maintenance triages a damage report and assigns a tech
goal:
The maintenance manager reviews the damage report, sets priority,
and assigns a tech. The kanban / scheduling board reflects this.
preconditions: A damage report exists against an asset, with description, severity, and (optionally) photos, and a maintenance ticket has been created in the maintenance queue. (Established by P5-DAMAGE-001.)

steps.action: Open the maintenance queue. Find the new ticket.
steps.expected:
Ticket visible with severity, asset, description, and
reporter.

steps.action:
Set priority: High (blocks production). Assign to a tech.
Schedule for today.
steps.expected:
Ticket updates with assignment. The tech sees it on their
dashboard.

expected_overall: Ticket triaged and dispatched.
pass_criteria: Priority set AND tech assigned AND tech sees the ticket.

negative_variants.title: Cannot assign ticket to a deactivated tech
negative_variants.action:
Try to assign the ticket to a maintenance tech who has been
deactivated.
negative_variants.expected:
The user is filtered out or the assignment is blocked with a
"user inactive" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Inactive tech cannot be assigned.

title: Maintenance tech executes the repair
goal:
Tech does the work, consumes any parts, records time, and closes
the ticket.
preconditions: A maintenance ticket has been triaged: priority is set, a tech is assigned, and the ticket is scheduled for execution. (Established by P5-DAMAGE-002.)

steps.action: Open the assigned ticket. Scan or click "Start work."
steps.expected: Time tracking begins.

steps.action:
Issue any required parts (from inventory, e.g., a hydraulic
fitting and a quart of fluid). Record consumed quantities.
steps.expected: Inventory decreases. Parts cost is recorded against the ticket.

steps.action:
Add a completion note: what was done, root cause if known,
recommended preventative action.
steps.expected: Note saves with timestamp.

steps.action: Close the ticket.
steps.expected:
Ticket closed. Asset returned to operable status. Total cost
(labor + parts) recorded against the asset's lifetime cost.

expected_overall: Repair is complete and costed. Asset is back to operable.
pass_criteria: Ticket closed AND parts and labor recorded AND asset back online.

negative_variants.title: Cannot close ticket without recording any labor or parts
negative_variants.action:
Try to close the maintenance ticket with no labor entries and
no parts consumed.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; closure with empty work record is
almost always a data-entry omission.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty closure refused or surfaces an explicit warning.

negative_variants.title: Cannot consume parts not on hand
negative_variants.action: Try to issue 5 hydraulic fittings when only 2 are on hand.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "insufficient on-hand" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-issue refused.

title: Run period-end FX revaluation on open foreign-currency balances
goal:
Verify the application revalues open foreign-currency AR and AP
balances at the period-end rate, posting an unrealized gain or
loss to the appropriate GL account.
preconditions: At least one open AR or AP balance in a foreign currency exists.
A period-end FX rate that differs from the original transaction rate is loaded.

steps.action:
Find the FX revaluation area. Run revaluation for the period
end.
steps.expected:
Application identifies open foreign-currency balances and
computes the revaluation per balance.

steps.action: Review the revaluation summary.
steps.expected:
For each balance: foreign amount, original USD value, revalued
USD value, unrealized gain or loss.

steps.action: Post the revaluation.
steps.expected:
Adjusting JE posts to AR / AP control accounts and to
Unrealized FX Gain / Loss (NOT Realized — that's reserved for
settlement).

expected_overall: FX revaluation posts unrealized gain / loss correctly.
pass_criteria:
Revaluation amount matches hand math AND unrealized account is
used (not realized).

title: Approve pay-period time and produce a payroll-export feed
goal:
Verify the time-to-payroll workflow: time entries are approved by
the manager, the system computes regular vs. overtime hours per
classification, and a payroll feed (file or integration) is produced
matching the approved data.
preconditions: Multiple labor entries from at least 2 employees in the pay period exist (P4-LABOR).
At least one employee is non-exempt (overtime eligible).

steps.action:
As the manager, open the time-approval queue for the pay
period. Approve all pending entries.
steps.expected: Approvals save.

steps.action:
For a non-exempt employee with > 40 hrs in the week, verify
the system splits hours into regular and overtime per the
configured rule.
steps.expected: OT hours are computed correctly (e.g., FLSA 40-hour rule).

steps.action: Generate the payroll feed (file export or integration push).
steps.expected:
File / payload contains every approved employee, regular hours,
OT hours, pay rate, and gross pay.

steps.action: Spot-check one row against the source.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: Time approves cleanly and payroll feed reflects approved data.
pass_criteria:
Approvals complete AND OT computed correctly AND feed matches
source.

title: Employee submits a PTO request; manager approves; balance updates
goal:
Verify employees can request PTO, managers approve / deny, the
balance updates correctly, and approved PTO appears on the
scheduling / capacity view so planners avoid over-booking.
preconditions: At least one employee with a configured PTO accrual policy exists.

steps.action:
As the employee, find the PTO area. Submit a request: 2 days
next month.
steps.expected:
Request saves in "Pending" status. Available PTO balance is
visible.

steps.action: As the manager, open the PTO approval queue. Approve.
steps.expected: Status changes to "Approved." The balance reservation updates.

steps.action: Open the production capacity view for the requested days.
steps.expected:
The employee's availability shows as "On PTO" and they're not
assignable on those days.

steps.action: Once the dates pass, balance is debited.
steps.expected: PTO balance reduces by 2 days.

expected_overall:
PTO request → approval → balance update → scheduling integration
works end-to-end.
pass_criteria:
Approval flow complete AND balance debited AND scheduling reflects
PTO.

title: Build a shift schedule and confirm employees see their shifts
goal:
Verify the shift management workflow: define shifts, assign
employees to shifts on a schedule, and have employees see their
upcoming shifts in their own view.
preconditions: At least 3 employees and at least 2 shift definitions exist (P1-CAL-001, P1-CAL-002).

steps.action:
Find the shift schedule area. Build a 1-week schedule covering
both first-shift and second-shift slots; assign 3 employees
across them.
steps.expected: Schedule saves. The view shows the shift assignments visually.

steps.action:
Sign in as one of the assigned employees. Open their schedule
view.
steps.expected: The employee sees their assigned shifts for the upcoming week.

steps.action: As the manager, swap two employees on a single shift.
steps.expected: Swap saves. Both employees see the updated assignment.

expected_overall: Shift scheduling propagates correctly to employee views.
pass_criteria:
Schedule saved AND employees see assignments AND swaps update
cleanly.

title: Terminate an employee with full offboarding workflow
goal:
Verify employee termination disables system access, retains
historical attribution (labor entries, audit log), produces final
pay and final-day documentation, and prevents new task / time
assignment.
preconditions: At least one employee with an active linked user account exists.

steps.action:
Open the employee record. Initiate termination. Capture
effective date, reason, and any final-pay items.
steps.expected: Termination form accepts.

steps.action: Confirm.
steps.expected:
Effective date applied. The linked user account is deactivated.
The employee record transitions to "Terminated" but is preserved.

steps.action: Try to sign in as the terminated user.
steps.expected: Sign-in is rejected.

steps.action: Try to assign a WO to the terminated employee.
steps.expected: Assignment is blocked with "employee terminated" message.

steps.action:
Run the labor distribution report covering the period before
termination.
steps.expected: The terminated employee's prior labor history is intact.

expected_overall:
Termination disables access, gates new assignment, preserves
history.
pass_criteria:
Sign-in blocked AND assignment blocked AND history preserved AND
audit recorded.

title: Record training and certifications with expiry on an employee
goal:
Verify training and certification records on an employee: capture
course / cert, completion date, expiry date, and ensure the
expiring-soon list surfaces upcoming renewals.
preconditions: At least one employee exists.

steps.action:
Open the employee. Add training records:
- "Forklift certification" — completed 2025-01-15, expires
  2027-01-15
- "Lockout / Tagout" — completed 2025-06-01, expires 2026-06-01
steps.expected: Both records save with their dates.

steps.action:
Run the "expiring within 60 days" report (or filter the
training list).
steps.expected:
The Lockout/Tagout cert appears (within 60 days of expiry as
of today's date if test date is in 2026-Q2).

steps.action:
Try to assign the employee to a work center that requires
forklift cert (if such a constraint is configured).
steps.expected: Assignment is allowed (cert valid).

steps.action:
Backdate the forklift cert to expired. Re-attempt the
assignment.
steps.expected: Assignment is blocked or warned.

expected_overall:
Training / cert records surface expirations and gate work
assignment where required.
pass_criteria:
Records saved AND expiring-soon report correct AND assignment
gating respects validity.

title: Manual inventory adjustment with a reason code
goal:
When inventory needs adjustment outside a cycle count (damage,
shrinkage, found stock), a manual adjustment with a reason code
posts to the right variance account.
preconditions: Inventory exists for at least one part.

steps.action:
Open the inventory adjustment area. Adjust 5 ft of steel out
of stock with reason: "Damaged in handling."
steps.expected: Adjustment is accepted. Reason is required.

steps.action:
Verify GL impact: inventory account decreased, scrap / handling
variance account increased.
steps.expected: Postings target the right variance account based on the reason.

expected_overall:
Inventory adjustments are reason-coded and post to the right
variance account.
pass_criteria:
Inventory adjusted AND reason captured AND GL postings reflect the
reason.

negative_variants.title: Reject adjustment that would drive on-hand negative
negative_variants.action: Try to adjust out 200 units when only 50 are on hand.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "would drive on-hand negative"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative on-hand prevention.

negative_variants.title: Reject adjustment without reason code
negative_variants.action: Try to save an adjustment with the reason field blank.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "reason is required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Adjustment without reason refused.

negative_variants.title: Large adjustments require controller approval
negative_variants.action:
Try to adjust out a value above the documented self-approval
threshold.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or routes for controller approval.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Large adjustments are gated by an approval workflow.

title: Post a balanced manual journal entry
goal:
Verify the manual JE workflow: enter a multi-line entry, ensure
debits = credits, post, and confirm GL impact.
preconditions: The chart of accounts is initialized.
An open period exists.

steps.action:
Find the JE entry area. Create a new JE with three lines:
- Debit Expense Account A: $300
- Debit Expense Account B: $200
- Credit Cash: $500
steps.expected: Entry accepts. Total debits = total credits = $500.

steps.action: Try to save with debits ≠ credits (e.g., change one line).
steps.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "must balance" message.

steps.action: Restore balance and post the entry.
steps.expected: JE posts. Each account's running balance updates.

steps.action: Open the audit log.
steps.expected: JE posting recorded with actor, timestamp, JE number, and total.

expected_overall:
Manual JEs post when balanced, are blocked when not, and are
audited.
pass_criteria: Entry posts when balanced AND blocked otherwise AND audit recorded.

title: Configure a recurring JE that posts each period
goal:
Verify a recurring template (e.g., monthly rent accrual) can be
defined and the system posts an instance each period — manually or
automatically.
preconditions: The chart of accounts is initialized.

steps.action:
Find the recurring JE area. Create a recurring entry: "Monthly
rent accrual" — Debit Rent Expense $5,000, Credit Accrued Rent
$5,000, monthly on the 1st, ending in 12 months.
steps.expected: Recurring template saves.

steps.action: Advance to the first scheduled date (or trigger manually).
steps.expected:
A JE is generated and posted (or queued, per workflow). It
references the recurring template.

steps.action: Open the recurring entry's history.
steps.expected: One generated JE so far, with timestamp.

expected_overall:
Recurring JEs generate per schedule with traceability to the
template.
pass_criteria:
Template saved AND first instance posted / queued AND traceability
back to template.

title: Configure a JE to auto-reverse in the next period
goal:
Verify a JE can be marked auto-reversing — useful for accruals.
When the next period opens, an offsetting reversal posts
automatically.
preconditions: At least two consecutive periods are configured.

steps.action:
Post a JE with the auto-reverse flag enabled, dated the last
day of the current period.
steps.expected:
JE posts. The reversal is scheduled for the first day of the
next period.

steps.action: Open the reversal queue (or advance to the next period).
steps.expected:
Reversal posts on the first day of the next period with
offsetting amounts. It references the original JE.

steps.action:
Verify GL impact: the original JE and the reversal net to zero
from the next period onward.
steps.expected: Net = 0.

expected_overall: Auto-reversing JEs work correctly.
pass_criteria: Original posts AND reversal posts on the right date AND net = 0.

title: Receive material back from the subcontractor
goal:
Material returns from the subcontractor; the routing resumes from
the operation after the subcontract.
preconditions: A sub-assembly has been shipped out for subcontract processing: a shipper / pack list was generated and the material is flagged offsite-with-vendor (no longer in available inventory). (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action: At receiving, scan the inbound shipper from the subcontractor.
steps.expected: System matches against the offsite WO. Material is reconciled.

steps.action:
Confirm receipt. Verify the WO automatically advances past the
subcontract operation.
steps.expected: WO continues at the next routing operation.

steps.action:
The subcontract cost is recorded against the WO (per the
routing's cost from P2-ROUTE-002).
steps.expected: WO actual cost increases by the subcontract cost.

expected_overall:
Subcontract round-trip is closed. WO continues with no manual
intervention beyond the receipt.
pass_criteria: Material received AND WO advanced AND subcontract cost recorded.

title: Ship a sub-assembly out for subcontract processing
goal:
When a routing has a subcontracted operation, the system handles
shipping out, holding while off-site, and receiving back.
preconditions: A WO is at a subcontract operation (from P2-ROUTE-002).

steps.action:
At the operation, scan to ship to the subcontractor. System
generates a shipper / pack list.
steps.expected:
Pack list and shipper are created. Material is marked as
offsite-with-vendor.

steps.action:
Verify that the material is no longer counted in available
inventory but appears in an "in-transit / offsite" report.
steps.expected: Material shows as offsite, not on-hand.

expected_overall:
Subcontracted material is properly tracked while away from the
facility.
pass_criteria:
Material flagged offsite AND shipper generated AND inventory
visibility maintained.

title: Configure a preventative maintenance schedule
goal:
Define a PM schedule on a critical asset — every N hours of run
time, or every N days, or both — so the system can trigger
preventative work before damage happens.
preconditions: At least one asset (P1-ASSET-001).

steps.action: Open the press asset. Find the PM section.
steps.expected: A way to define PM schedules is present.

steps.action:
Add a schedule:
- Type: Quarterly (every 90 days)
- Description: "Hydraulic fluid change + filter inspection"
- Required parts: hydraulic fluid, filter
- Estimated labor: 2 hours
steps.expected: Schedule saves.

steps.action:
Set the next due date and verify the system will create a PM WO
automatically when due.
steps.expected:
Next-due date is visible. A scheduled job will create the WO
when the date arrives.

expected_overall:
PM schedule is configured. The system will trigger work before
damage occurs.
pass_criteria:
Schedule exists AND next-due is visible AND scheduled trigger
is in place.

negative_variants.title: Reject PM schedule with zero or negative interval
negative_variants.action:
Try to configure a PM schedule with interval = 0 days or
runtime hours = -100.
negative_variants.expected: Submission is blocked with a clear error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Zero / negative intervals rejected.

title: Trigger a PM work order manually and complete it
goal:
Verify the manual trigger path for PMs (e.g., advancing schedule
for a planned shutdown) and that execution mirrors damage repair.
preconditions: A preventative maintenance schedule is configured on an asset, with interval, description, required parts, estimated labor, and a next-due date that will trigger a PM work order. (Established by P5-PM-001.)

steps.action: From the press's PM section, manually trigger the next PM.
steps.expected:
A PM work order is created and assigned (or queued for
assignment).

steps.action:
As the assigned tech, execute the PM: parts, labor, completion
note.
steps.expected:
PM completes. Schedule advances — next due date moves forward
by the configured interval.

expected_overall: PM cycle is closed. Schedule advances automatically.
pass_criteria: PM WO closed AND parts/labor recorded AND next-due date advanced.

negative_variants.title: Cannot trigger duplicate PM while one is open
negative_variants.action:
Trigger a PM. Before it is closed, try to trigger the same PM
again.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or warns; only one open PM per schedule
should be active.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate active PM refused.

title: Open a CAPA from an NCR and track to closure
goal:
Verify the CAPA workflow: from a recurring NCR pattern, open a
CAPA, assign owner, capture root cause and corrective action,
define preventive action, and close with verification.
preconditions: At least one NCR exists (P5-QC-NCR-001).

steps.action:
From an NCR (or independently), open a CAPA. Set:
- Description: short summary of the issue
- Owner: assigned engineer
- Due date: 30 days from today
steps.expected: CAPA saves with a stable ID, in "Open" status.

steps.action:
As the owner, document root cause analysis (e.g., "Vendor's
lot heat-treatment was off-spec") and corrective action ("Reject
lot, request vendor re-test"). Save.
steps.expected: RCA and CA saved.

steps.action:
Document preventive action ("Add incoming hardness check to
inspection plan for steel from this vendor").
steps.expected: PA saved.

steps.action:
Close the CAPA with a verification step (e.g., "Updated plan
P5-QC-INSPECT-001 with hardness check, verified active 2026-04-25").
steps.expected:
CAPA transitions to Closed. Closure includes verification text
and date.

expected_overall: CAPA captures the full Plan-Do-Check-Act loop with audit trail.
pass_criteria: CAPA opened AND RCA / CA / PA all captured AND closure verified.
why_this_matters:
CAPAs that don't track to verified closure are theater. Auditors
for ISO / AS9100 specifically check that closures have evidence.

title: Generate a certificate of analysis (COA) for a shipped lot
goal:
Verify the system can generate a COA for a customer shipment that
ties to the actual lot's inspection results — not a generic
template.
preconditions: At least one lot-tracked finished good was inspected and shipped on a customer SO.

steps.action:
Open a shipped SO that contained a lot-tracked product. Find
the COA generation action.
steps.expected: COA generation is available, scoped to the actual lot(s) shipped.

steps.action: Generate the COA.
steps.expected:
COA document includes: customer, SO, lot number(s), inspection
results from the lot's QC record (not a generic boilerplate),
authorized signature line.

expected_overall: COA reflects the actual shipped lot's inspection data.
pass_criteria:
COA generated AND lot-specific data present AND no generic / placeholder
values in inspection sections.

title: A QC inspection fails — initiate rework
goal:
At a QC step in a production routing, parts fail inspection. They
loop back through the routing for rework rather than going to
finished goods.
preconditions: A WO is at an inspection operation.

steps.action:
At inspection, scan the parts. Mark 3 of the 100 as failed.
Reason: "Weld porosity."
steps.expected:
Failure is recorded. The 3 parts move to a rework status, not
to finished goods.

steps.action: Choose the rework path: send back to Weld for repair.
steps.expected:
System creates a rework operation back at Weld for the 3 units.
The original WO continues with 97 units still moving forward.

steps.action: Verify the rework operation is visible to the Weld work center.
steps.expected: Rework is on Weld's queue.

expected_overall:
QC failures route to rework with full traceability of which units
were reworked at which operations.
pass_criteria:
Failed units flagged AND routed to rework AND traceable AND main
WO continues for the good units.

negative_variants.title: Reject QC fail without reason code
negative_variants.action: Mark units failed with the reason code blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "QC fail reason is required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Failure without reason refused.

negative_variants.title: Failed units cannot move to finished goods
negative_variants.action:
After marking units failed, try to manually move them into
finished goods inventory.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a "failed units cannot enter FG"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Failed units stay isolated from sellable inventory.

title: Track gage calibration; out-of-cal gages cannot be used for inspection
goal:
Verify that measurement devices (calipers, micrometers, CMMs, etc.)
have calibration records; using an out-of-cal gage for inspection
is blocked or flagged.
preconditions: The inspection plan from P5-QC-INSPECT-001 references measurement tools.

steps.action:
Find the gage / device area. Create a gage record:
- ID: GAGE-CAL-001
- Type: digital caliper
- Last cal: 6 months ago
- Next cal due: 6 months from last cal
steps.expected: Gage saves.

steps.action:
Verify the gage's status shows "Calibrated" with the next-due
date.
steps.expected: Status correct.

steps.action:
Backdate the next-cal-due to yesterday (so the gage is now
out-of-cal). Try to use the gage on an inspection.
steps.expected:
The application blocks or warns clearly that the gage is
out-of-calibration. The user cannot silently use it.

steps.action: Record a calibration event with current date and certificate.
steps.expected: Status returns to "Calibrated." Next-due rolls forward.

expected_overall: Gage calibration is tracked and out-of-cal usage is gated.
pass_criteria:
Gage status accurate AND out-of-cal blocks / warns AND new cal
event resets status.

title: Create an inspection plan and apply it to vendor receipts
goal:
Define an inspection plan for a part (sample size, tests, accept
criteria) and verify that incoming receipts of the part trigger an
inspection task before stock becomes available.
preconditions: At least one purchased raw material exists.

steps.action:
Find the inspection plans area. Create a plan for
RM-STEEL-1018-3X3:
- Sample: 5% of receipt, minimum 5
- Tests: dimensional check, visual surface check
- Accept criteria: zero rejections in sample
steps.expected: Plan saves and is associated with the part.

steps.action: Receive a PO for the part (per P3-RECV-001 flow).
steps.expected:
Stock arrives in a "pending inspection" status, NOT directly
available.

steps.action:
Open the QC inspection queue. Pick up the inspection task.
Record the dimensional check and visual check as pass.
steps.expected: Inspection completes. Stock moves from pending to available.

steps.action: Repeat with a sample failure.
steps.expected: Stock either stays in quarantine or routes to NCR (P5-QC-NCR-001).

expected_overall: Inspection plans gate stock availability based on real QC outcomes.
pass_criteria:
Plan applied AND stock gated AND pass / fail outcomes drive
disposition.

title: Open an NCR on rejected material and route for disposition
goal:
Verify that material failing inspection generates a non-conformance
report capturing the defect, that the NCR routes for disposition
(rework / scrap / use-as-is / RTV), and that the chosen disposition
drives the inventory and GL outcome.
preconditions: At least one inspection has failed (P5-QC-INSPECT-001).

steps.action:
From a failed inspection, create an NCR. Capture: part, lot,
quantity, defect description, photos.
steps.expected: NCR saves with a stable ID. Affected stock is in quarantine.

steps.action:
Route to MRB (Material Review Board) or equivalent. Record a
disposition: "Use as-is" with engineer signoff.
steps.expected: Disposition saves with the engineer's name and timestamp.

steps.action:
Verify the disposition is acted on: stock moves out of
quarantine to available (use-as-is) or to scrap, return-to-
vendor, etc.
steps.expected:
Inventory state matches disposition. GL impact (if scrap or
RTV) posts to the right account.

expected_overall:
NCR captures the issue, drives a disposition, and the disposition
is enacted.
pass_criteria:
NCR exists AND disposition recorded with signoff AND inventory /
GL match disposition.

title: Floor operator records feedback against an engineering revision
goal:
Floor operator notices an issue with a part or assembly during
production. They log feedback against the engineering revision so
R&D sees it.
preconditions: At least one BOM with a revision (P2-BOM-003).

steps.action:
At the work center, while running a WO, open the part being
produced. Find a "feedback to engineering" or "redline" action.
steps.expected: Action is available, visible to the floor.

steps.action:
Submit feedback: "Tab on bracket interferes with assembly when
bolt heads are oversized — recommend chamfer the inside corner."
Optionally attach a photo.
steps.expected: Feedback saves and routes to engineering.

steps.action:
As engineering, see the feedback in their queue, linked to the
part / revision.
steps.expected:
Feedback is visible. Engineering can acknowledge, escalate to
ECR, or close.

expected_overall:
Floor-to-engineering feedback loop works. R&D has visibility into
what's failing on the production floor.
pass_criteria:
Feedback saved AND linked to the right part / revision AND visible
to engineering.

title: Trace all finished products built from a specific raw material lot
goal:
When a raw material lot is suspect (vendor-issued recall, internal
quality issue), find every finished good built using that lot.
preconditions: At least one lot-tracked raw material has been issued to a WO (P4-MATL-ISSUE).

steps.action: Search by the lot number "PSS-2026-04-001."
steps.expected:
System shows: original receipt, work orders that consumed it,
finished units produced, customers shipped to.

steps.action: Generate a recall report for that lot.
steps.expected: Report lists all customers and shipped quantities affected.

expected_overall:
Lot traceability holds end-to-end: raw material → WO → FG →
customer.
pass_criteria:
Lot trace produces all impacted customers AND quantities AND
serial numbers (if applicable).
why_this_matters:
This is the single most expensive thing to get wrong. A failed lot
trace turns a targeted recall into a "recall everything we shipped
in a window" — orders of magnitude more cost.

title: Customer initiates a return (RMA)
goal:
Customer reports a problem with a shipped product. RMA is created
and the customer is told how to ship it back.
preconditions: At least one shipped invoice exists (P4-SHIP / P4-INV-001).

steps.action:
Find the RMAs area. Create a new RMA referencing the original
invoice / order.
steps.expected:
RMA form opens. Lines and prices pre-fill from the original
shipment.

steps.action:
Fill in: returned quantity, reason ("dimensional out-of-spec"),
desired resolution (replace / repair / refund).
steps.expected: RMA saves with a stable number.

steps.action:
Generate an RMA confirmation for the customer with shipping
instructions.
steps.expected:
Confirmation includes the RMA number, return address, and any
shipping label or instructions.

expected_overall: RMA is created and the customer has return instructions.
pass_criteria:
RMA exists AND has a stable number AND the customer document
includes return shipping instructions.

negative_variants.title: Reject RMA quantity exceeding shipped quantity
negative_variants.action: Try to RMA 200 units when the original shipment was 100.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "exceeds shipped quantity"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-RMA refused.

negative_variants.title: RMA outside warranty / return window requires override
negative_variants.action: Reference an invoice older than the documented return window.
negative_variants.expected: The save is blocked or warns and requires explicit override.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Out-of-window RMA is gated.

title: Receive returned product
goal: Physically receive the returned product, inspect, and route it.
preconditions: An RMA has been created against a shipped invoice, with returned quantity, reason, desired resolution, and customer-facing return instructions. (Established by P5-RMA-001.)

steps.action: At receiving, scan the RMA number on the inbound package.
steps.expected: The expected RMA opens.

steps.action: Receive the returned units. Inspect them.
steps.expected:
Each unit is recorded as received. QC outcome can be set per
unit (return-to-stock, rework, scrap).

steps.action: Route 5 units to scrap (unsalvageable), 5 to rework.
steps.expected:
Scrap and rework destinations are set. Inventory updates
accordingly (no scrap units back to FG inventory).

expected_overall:
Returned product is in the right disposition: scrap, rework, or
return-to-stock based on inspection.
pass_criteria:
All units accounted for AND each routed to its disposition AND
inventory not contaminated with bad stock.

negative_variants.title: Reject return quantity exceeding RMA quantity
negative_variants.action: Receive 15 units against an RMA authorizing 10.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or surfaces an explicit prompt to amend the
RMA before accepting the overage.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-return is gated.

negative_variants.title: Return-to-stock disposition requires QC pass
negative_variants.action:
Try to route units to return-to-stock without recording any
QC outcome.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "QC outcome required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: RTS without QC refused.

title: Issue a credit memo against the returned invoice
goal:
Settle the financial side of the RMA — credit the customer for the
returned units, reducing AR.
preconditions: Returned product has been physically received, inspected, and dispositioned — units routed to return-to-stock, rework, or scrap as appropriate. (Established by P5-RMA-002.)

steps.action:
Create a credit memo from the RMA. It pre-fills with the
returned quantities and prices.
steps.expected: Credit memo draft appears.

steps.action: Confirm and post.
steps.expected:
Credit memo posts. AR decreases. GL impact: revenue reduced,
AR credited.

steps.action:
Apply the credit memo to the original invoice (or, if invoice
is paid, leave as customer credit).
steps.expected: Credit memo applied appropriately.

expected_overall: Customer's account is settled.
pass_criteria:
Credit memo posted AND applied or held as credit AND AR/revenue
postings correct.

negative_variants.title: Reject credit memo exceeding original invoice amount
negative_variants.action:
Try to post a credit memo for $10,000 against an invoice with
a $5,000 total.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or warns; over-credit must be explicit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-credit gated.

negative_variants.title: Cannot apply credit memo to a different customer
negative_variants.action:
Try to apply ACME's credit memo against another customer's
invoice.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "credit applies only to the
issuing customer" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Cross-customer credit refused.

title: Floor reports a work stoppage with reason
goal:
When work stops at a station for any reason — material shortage,
asset down, no work — the operator records why so reporting can
identify common stoppage causes.
preconditions: A WO is in progress.

steps.action:
At the station, choose "Report stoppage" or scan the stoppage
barcode.
steps.expected: A reason picker appears.

steps.action: Choose: "Material shortage — RM-STEEL-1018-3X3 ran out."
steps.expected: Stoppage starts. Time tracking changes from labor to stoppage.

steps.action: Once material arrives, end the stoppage and resume work.
steps.expected:
Time-on-stoppage is recorded against the WO and the reason
code. Labor resumes.

expected_overall:
Stoppages are tracked with reason codes. Reports can identify the
most common stoppage causes.
pass_criteria:
Stoppage time captured AND reason coded AND labor / stoppage time
separable in reports.

negative_variants.title: Reject stoppage with no reason chosen
negative_variants.action: Try to start a stoppage without selecting a reason code.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "reason required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reason-less stoppage refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot end a stoppage that was never started
negative_variants.action: Try to end a stoppage on an operation with no open stoppage.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is unavailable or surfaces a clear "no active
stoppage" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: End-without-start refused.

title: Track VAT on transactions and produce a VAT return for the period
goal:
Verify that for a tenant in a VAT jurisdiction, VAT is collected
and recoverable on the right transactions, posts to a VAT
payable / receivable account, and a VAT return summary can be
produced.
preconditions: The tenant or a sub-entity has a VAT registration configured.
At least one customer invoice with VAT and one vendor invoice with VAT exist in the period.

steps.action:
Verify a VAT-bearing customer invoice posts: revenue, VAT
collected (liability), AR.
steps.expected: GL postings correct.

steps.action:
Verify a VAT-bearing vendor invoice posts: expense, VAT
recoverable (asset), AP.
steps.expected: GL postings correct.

steps.action: Run the VAT return for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows total VAT collected, total recoverable, net VAT
payable / refundable. Numbers reconcile to the underlying
transactions.

expected_overall: VAT is tracked through transactions and producible as a return.
pass_criteria:
VAT collected and recoverable post to correct accounts AND return
reconciles to source.

title: Return defective material to a vendor
goal:
Process a return-to-vendor (RTV) for material rejected on receipt
or discovered defective in production.
preconditions: Some quarantined or defective material exists from P3-RECV-003 or similar.

steps.action:
Find the RTV area. Create an RTV against Pacific Steel Supply
for 50 ft of defective steel.
steps.expected: RTV draft is created.

steps.action:
Enter reason and submit. Generate vendor-facing documentation
(RMA-equivalent for the vendor).
steps.expected: RTV document is ready to attach to the shipment back.

steps.action:
Once shipped, mark the RTV as shipped. Quarantined inventory
decreases.
steps.expected:
Inventory updates. Vendor credit is expected on the next AP
cycle.

expected_overall: Defective material flows back to the vendor with proper paperwork.
pass_criteria: RTV created AND quarantine cleared AND vendor expected to issue credit.

negative_variants.title: Reject RTV quantity exceeding quarantined inventory
negative_variants.action: Try to RTV 100 ft when only 50 ft is quarantined.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "exceeds quarantined quantity"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-RTV refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot ship RTV without vendor authorization number
negative_variants.action:
Try to ship the RTV without entering the vendor-issued RMA /
authorization number.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; vendors typically require an
authorization number to accept the return.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: RTV without vendor authorization is gated.

title: Process a warranty claim using a serialized product
goal:
Customer reports a warranty issue on a serialized product. The
system finds the serial, identifies the original sale and BOM
revision, and processes the claim.
preconditions: At least one serial-tracked part has been shipped (P2-PART-004, P4-SHIP).

steps.action:
In the warranty / RMA area, search for a serial number that
was shipped to a customer.
steps.expected:
System finds the serial, displays the original SO, ship date,
customer, and BOM revision in effect at production.

steps.action: Initiate a warranty claim. Capture the issue description.
steps.expected:
Claim is created and linked to the serial. The original BOM
revision is preserved as part of the claim record.

expected_overall:
Serial-traceable warranty support works end-to-end with original
production data preserved.
pass_criteria: Serial found AND original SO linked AND BOM revision captured.
why_this_matters:
Without BOM revision traceability, a warranty claim against a unit
produced 2 years ago is impossible to investigate — you don't
know which version of the design that unit was.

title: Withhold tax on a vendor payment and remit to the authority
goal:
Verify the application supports withholding tax: a vendor payment
has a portion withheld and posted to a withholding-payable account,
the vendor sees only the net payment, and the withheld amount is
trackable for remittance to the tax authority.
preconditions: At least one vendor is flagged for withholding (e.g., a foreign contractor).
An open invoice from that vendor exists.

steps.action:
Open the vendor record. Configure withholding: e.g., 30% on
services.
steps.expected: Withholding setting saves.

steps.action: Pay the vendor's open invoice.
steps.expected:
Payment posts net of withholding (e.g., $1,000 invoice ->
$700 to vendor, $300 to withholding payable).

steps.action: Run the withholding-payable balance.
steps.expected: Balance equals withheld amounts pending remittance.

expected_overall: Withholding posts correctly and is trackable for remittance.
pass_criteria:
Net payment to vendor AND withholding payable balance correct AND
remittance can be reported.

title: Administrator is denied approving a journal entry
goal:
Verify the Administrator cannot approve pending JEs. Approval is a
financial control belonging to Controller / Finance.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one over-threshold JE awaiting approval exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Administrator. Look for the JE approval queue.
steps.expected: The approval queue is not visible or not enabled.

steps.action: Attempt the approve-JE endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot approve a JE.
pass_criteria: No JE approved by this user AND endpoint rejected.

title: Administrator is denied closing a fiscal period
goal:
Verify the Administrator role cannot close a fiscal period. Period
close is a financial action that belongs to Controller; the
Administrator owns tenant configuration, not finance.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one open fiscal period exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Search the navigation for a
period-close action.
steps.expected: No close-period action is reachable from the UI for this role.

steps.action: Type the period-close URL directly into the address bar.
steps.expected:
The page does not render the close action. Permission denial or
redirect.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the close-period call.
steps.expected:
The request is rejected with an authorization error. The period
is not closed.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot close a fiscal period.
pass_criteria:
Period not closed AND UI denies access AND direct URL blocked AND
API rejects the request.

title: Administrator is allowed to modify role permissions
goal:
Verify the Administrator (the founding tenant user) can also
configure role permissions, supporting initial setup before an IT
Admin user is created and as escalation backup later.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists.
At least one editable, non-system role exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Open role configuration. Toggle one
capability on a custom role.
steps.expected: Change saves.

steps.action: Open the role-config audit log.
steps.expected:
Change is attributed to the Administrator with timestamp, the
role modified, and prior / new permissions.

expected_overall: Administrator modifies role permissions; change is fully auditable.
pass_criteria:
Role permissions updated AND audit log captures actor, timestamp,
role, and before / after permission set.

title: Administrator is allowed to create a system user
goal:
Verify the Administrator (the founding tenant user) can create
additional users in early-stage operation, and that the action is
audited.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists.
At least one role exists to assign to a new user.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Open the user management area. Create
a new user with a test email and a non-admin role.
steps.expected: User is created. Invitation or temporary credential is generated.

steps.action: Open the audit log.
steps.expected:
User creation is attributed to the Administrator with timestamp
and the role granted.

expected_overall: Administrator creates users; action is auditable.
pass_criteria:
User created AND audit captures actor, target, role granted,
timestamp.
notes:
In larger orgs IT Admin owns user management; in smaller orgs the
founding Administrator does. Both allow paths exist.

title: Administrator is allowed to delete an unreferenced customer record
goal:
Verify the Administrator can permanently delete a customer record
that has no transactional history (no quotes, sales orders,
invoices, payments, or shipments), and that the deletion is
recorded in the audit log.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists.
At least one customer record exists with no transactional references (e.g., a misspelled draft customer never used).

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Open the customer record. Find the
delete action.
steps.expected:
The delete action is enabled because the customer has no
transactional references.

steps.action: Delete the customer. Confirm any irreversibility prompt.
steps.expected: The customer is removed from the active customer list.

steps.action: Open the audit log.
steps.expected:
Deletion is attributed to the Administrator with timestamp,
customer name / id, and a snapshot of the deleted record.

expected_overall: Administrator deletes an unreferenced customer; deletion auditable.
pass_criteria:
Customer removed AND audit log captures actor, timestamp, customer
identity, and a record snapshot.
notes:
A customer with any transactional history must NOT be deletable —
that's a separate negative-state case. This case asserts the
happy path on truly unreferenced records.

title: Administrator is denied issuing a customer credit memo
goal: Verify the Administrator cannot issue credit memos.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer with an open AR balance exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Look for any credit-memo issuance
surface.
steps.expected: The issuance surface is not visible or not enabled.

steps.action: Attempt the issue-credit-memo endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot issue a credit memo.
pass_criteria: No credit memo posted AND endpoint rejected.

title: Administrator is allowed to manage API keys and integrations
goal:
Verify the Administrator can manage integrations during setup
(P0 binds shipping / accounting integrations), and that key actions
are audited.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists.
The integrations / API key area is enabled.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Open the integrations area. Bind or
reconfigure one integration's credentials.
steps.expected:
Change saves. The integration is connected (or marked
reconnected).

steps.action: Open the integrations audit log.
steps.expected:
The action is attributed to the Administrator with timestamp,
integration name, and a key fingerprint / id (NOT the secret).

expected_overall: Administrator manages integrations; action is auditable.
pass_criteria:
Integration updated AND audit log captures actor, timestamp,
integration, and key id. Secret is NOT logged.

title: Administrator is denied changing a customer credit limit
goal:
Verify the Administrator cannot change customer credit limits. That
authority belongs to Controller / Finance.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer with an existing credit limit exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Open a customer record. Find the
credit limit field.
steps.expected: Field is read-only or not editable for this role.

steps.action:
Try to PATCH / submit a credit-limit update via direct URL or
API.
steps.expected: The update is rejected.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot change credit limits.
pass_criteria: Limit unchanged AND endpoint rejected.

title: Administrator is denied modifying tax codes
goal:
Verify the Administrator cannot edit tax-code rates or GL mappings.
Tax setup is a financial-control function that belongs to the
Controller; the Administrator owns tenant identity, not finance.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one tax code is configured.

steps.action: Sign in as Administrator. Open the tax-code configuration.
steps.expected:
The tax-code list is read-only or not visible. Edit affordances
on individual tax codes are disabled.

steps.action: Attempt to PATCH a tax-code rate via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot modify tax codes.
pass_criteria: Tax codes unchanged AND endpoint rejected.

title: Administrator is allowed to modify tenant settings
goal:
Verify an Administrator can change a tenant-level setting (e.g., a
display preference, NOT a settings change with severe downstream
effects) and the change persists with audit attribution.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists.
Tenant identity is configured.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Open company settings. Change a
reversible field, e.g., add a secondary phone number.
steps.expected: Change saves.

steps.action: Open the settings audit log.
steps.expected: Change is attributed to the Administrator with a timestamp.

expected_overall: Administrator modifies tenant; change is auditable.
pass_criteria: Setting changed AND audit log captures user / timestamp.

title: Administrator is denied posting a journal entry
goal:
Verify the Administrator role — which owns tenant identity and
integrations but not financial operations — cannot post manual
journal entries. Posting belongs to Controller.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists and can sign in with no other roles attached.
The chart of accounts is initialized.
At least one open fiscal period exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Administrator. Look in the navigation for any
journal-entries / accounting-posting surface.
steps.expected:
The JE area is either not visible to the Administrator, or is
visible read-only with no create / post action.

steps.action: Type the journal-entries URL directly into the address bar.
steps.expected:
The page does not render a JE creation form. A clear permission
denial appears, or the user is redirected to their home.

steps.action:
If an API endpoint is exposed, attempt the equivalent
"create journal entry" call with the Administrator's session.
steps.expected:
The request is rejected with a clear authorization error
(e.g., 403). No JE is created.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot post a JE through any path.
pass_criteria:
No JE created AND UI denies access AND direct URL is blocked AND
any API path returns an authorization error.
why_this_matters:
Segregation of duties: a tenant administrator who can also post
financial transactions defeats the auditability the segregation is
meant to provide. The Administrator role should be powerful but
bounded.

title: Administrator is denied posting a payroll run
goal:
Verify the Administrator cannot post payroll. Payroll is HR-bound;
tenant administration is a separate authority.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one prepared payroll run exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Administrator. Look for any payroll surface.
steps.expected: The payroll surface is read-only or not reachable.

steps.action: Attempt the post-payroll endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot post payroll.
pass_criteria: Run not posted AND attempt rejected.

title: Administrator is denied voiding a posted customer invoice
goal:
Verify the Administrator role cannot void a posted invoice. Voiding
is a financial reversal action that belongs to Controller; the
Administrator owns tenant configuration, not finance.
preconditions: The Administrator user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one posted customer invoice exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Administrator. Open a posted invoice.
steps.expected: The void / cancel action is hidden, disabled, or not available.

steps.action: Attempt the void endpoint via direct URL.
steps.expected: The action is rejected with an authorization error.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the void-invoice call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Administrator cannot void an invoice.
pass_criteria:
Invoice not voided AND no GL reversal posted AND attempt rejected
at every accessible surface.
why_this_matters:
Tenant administrators frequently get over-permissioned because
"they're admin, they should be able to do anything." For financial
reversals that defeats the audit story — the Administrator's
authority should stop at tenant configuration.

title: Controller is denied approving an engineering change request
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot approve ECRs. Cost-impact review may
involve Controller, but the formal Approve action remains with
Engineering.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one open ECR exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller. Open the ECR if visible.
steps.expected: Approve action is hidden or disabled.

steps.action: Attempt the approve endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Controller cannot approve an ECR.
pass_criteria: ECR not approved AND attempt rejected.

title: Controller is allowed to approve a journal entry above the threshold
goal:
Verify a Controller can approve a posted JE whose absolute amount
exceeds the approval-required threshold (so it sits in pending-
approval state), and that the approval is recorded with a separate
attribution from the original poster.
preconditions: A Controller user exists, distinct from the user who posted the pending JE.
At least one over-threshold JE awaiting approval exists, posted by a different user.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller (the approver). Open the pending JE.
steps.expected:
The JE shows as awaiting approval. The Approve action is
enabled. The original poster is shown.

steps.action: Approve the JE.
steps.expected:
The JE transitions to Posted-and-Approved. The GL impact is
recognized at the post date if it wasn't already.

steps.action: Open the JE audit log.
steps.expected:
Audit log lists both the poster (original timestamp) and the
approver (this Controller, separate timestamp).

expected_overall:
Controller approves a high-value JE; both poster and approver are
recorded.
pass_criteria:
JE approved AND audit log shows distinct poster and approver, both
with timestamps.
notes:
This case exercises the segregation-of-duties pattern even within
the Controller role: the *approver* must not be the *poster* on
the same entry.

title: Controller is allowed to approve a PO above procurement's threshold
goal:
Verify a Controller can approve a draft PO whose total exceeds the
procurement role's approval threshold, and that the approval is
recorded with attribution.
preconditions: A Controller user exists.
A draft PO exists with a total above procurement's threshold and routed for higher-tier approval.

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Open the draft PO awaiting higher-tier
approval.
steps.expected: The Approve action is enabled for this user.

steps.action: Approve the PO.
steps.expected: PO transitions to Approved (or Issued, per workflow).

steps.action: Open the PO history / audit log.
steps.expected:
Approval recorded with the Controller user, timestamp, and the
threshold tier that triggered higher-level approval.

expected_overall: Controller approves an over-threshold PO; approval auditable.
pass_criteria: PO approved AND audit shows approver, timestamp, and threshold tier.

title: Controller is allowed to close a fiscal period
goal:
Verify a Controller can lock a fiscal period and that the close
records who and when in the audit log.
preconditions: A Controller user exists and can sign in.
At least one fiscal period is in a state ready to close (no unposted required adjustments).

steps.action: Sign in as the Controller. Find the period close action.
steps.expected: The action is visible and enabled.

steps.action: Close the period. Confirm any prompts.
steps.expected: Period transitions to closed / locked.

steps.action: Open the audit log.
steps.expected:
The close action is recorded with Controller user, timestamp,
and the period closed.

expected_overall: Controller closes a period; the action is auditable.
pass_criteria: Period is closed AND audit log captures user / timestamp.

title: Controller is denied modifying role permissions
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot change role permission sets. Identity
/ authorization configuration belongs to IT Admin / Administrator;
a Controller who can also rewrite the permission model can
manufacture financial authority for themselves.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one editable role exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller. Look for any role-configuration area.
steps.expected: Role config is not visible or not enabled.

steps.action: Attempt the role-modify endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Controller cannot modify role permissions.
pass_criteria: Role permissions unchanged AND endpoint rejected.
why_this_matters:
Authorization configuration must be segregated from financial
operations. A Controller who can also configure roles can grant
themselves any financial capability that exists in the system.

title: Controller is denied creating a system user
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot create system users. Identity
administration belongs to IT Admin / Administrator; a Controller
who can also provision users could grant themselves capabilities.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller. Look for any user-management area.
steps.expected: User management is not visible or not enabled.

steps.action: Type the user-create URL directly.
steps.expected: The form does not render. Permission denial.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the create-user call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Controller cannot create users.
pass_criteria: No user created AND endpoint rejected.
why_this_matters:
A Controller who can also provision users can mint themselves
whatever access they want, defeating the segregation of finance
from identity.

title: Controller is denied permanently deleting a customer record
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot permanently delete customer records.
Controllers manage credit and AR but the lifecycle action of
deleting a master record belongs to Administrator.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer record with no transactional history exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Open the customer record. Look for a
delete action.
steps.expected: No delete action is available, or it is disabled.

steps.action: Attempt the delete endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Controller cannot delete a customer record.
pass_criteria: Customer record unchanged AND endpoint rejected.

title: Controller is allowed to issue a customer credit memo
goal:
Verify a Controller can issue a credit memo against a posted
invoice (or stand-alone), generate the offsetting GL entries, and
capture the action with reason and amount in the audit log.
preconditions: A Controller user exists.
At least one customer with an open AR balance exists.
The fiscal period for the credit memo's effective date is open.

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Open the customer's AR. Issue a $100
credit memo against an open invoice with reason "concession".
steps.expected:
Credit memo posts. AR balance is reduced by $100. Offsetting GL
entries (debit revenue / contra-revenue, credit AR) are recorded
on today's date.

steps.action: Open the credit-memo audit log.
steps.expected:
Action is attributed to the Controller with timestamp, amount,
reason, target invoice, and the resulting GL document.

expected_overall: Controller issues a credit memo; GL impact and audit are intact.
pass_criteria:
Credit memo posted AND GL entries created AND audit captures
actor, timestamp, amount, reason, and linked invoice.

title: Controller is denied managing API keys / integrations
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot create or rotate API keys. Integration
credential management belongs to IT Admin / Administrator; a
Controller who can also mint API keys can grant external services
arbitrary financial authority.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.
The integrations area exists and has at least one configured integration.

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Look for any API keys / integrations
area.
steps.expected: The integrations admin area is not visible or not enabled.

steps.action: Attempt a create-API-key endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Controller cannot manage API keys.
pass_criteria:
No new key created AND no existing keys rotated AND endpoint
rejected.

title: Controller is denied modifying a BOM
goal:
Verify the Controller role — which has broad financial authority
but no engineering authority — cannot change a BOM. Engineering
changes belong to Engineering.
preconditions: A Controller user exists.
A finished part has a released BOM.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller. Open the BOM. Examine the edit affordances.
steps.expected:
The BOM is read-only for this user. Edit / save controls are
disabled, hidden, or replaced with view-only.

steps.action: Attempt the BOM modify endpoint via direct URL.
steps.expected: The action is rejected. The BOM is unchanged.

expected_overall: Controller cannot modify a BOM.
pass_criteria: BOM is unchanged AND no new revision created.
notes:
This case probes a "powerful but bounded" role. The Controller having
every power except this one is the kind of nuance worth verifying.

title: Controller is allowed to change a customer credit limit
goal:
Verify the Controller can update a customer's credit limit and that
the change records cleanly in the audit log with prior and new
values.
preconditions: A Controller user exists.
At least one customer with an existing credit limit exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Open a customer record. Change the
credit limit from its current value to a different value. Save.
steps.expected: Change saves.

steps.action: Open the customer's audit log.
steps.expected:
The change is recorded with user, timestamp, prior value, and
new value.

expected_overall: Controller updates credit limit; change is fully auditable.
pass_criteria: Limit changed AND audit shows before / after / actor / timestamp.

title: Controller is allowed to modify tax-code rates and mappings
goal:
Verify a Controller can edit a tax code's rate or its GL mapping,
and that the change is captured with prior / new values for audit.
preconditions: A Controller user exists.
At least one tax code is configured (e.g., a state sales tax with a rate and a mapped GL liability account).

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Open the tax-code configuration. Open
one tax code and change its rate to a different value (e.g.,
from 7.5% to 7.75%) effective today.
steps.expected: Change saves. The new rate is now active.

steps.action: Open the tax-code audit log.
steps.expected:
The change is recorded with the Controller user, timestamp,
prior rate, new rate, and effective date.

expected_overall: Controller modifies a tax code; change is fully auditable.
pass_criteria:
Tax code updated AND audit log shows actor, timestamp, before /
after, and effective date.

title: Controller is denied modifying tenant identity / company settings
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot change tenant identity. Controllers own
the chart of accounts and financial calendar but not the tenant's
legal identity, time zone, or primary currency selection.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.
Tenant identity is configured.

steps.action:
Sign in as Controller. Look for the company / tenant settings
area.
steps.expected:
The company-settings area is either not visible, or read-only
on tenant identity fields. Financial-calendar adjacent fields
may remain editable separately.

steps.action:
Type the tenant settings URL directly and try editing legal
name or fiscal-year start.
steps.expected: Edits are rejected. Settings unchanged.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt to PATCH a tenant identity field.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Controller cannot modify tenant identity.
pass_criteria:
Tenant identity unchanged AND UI denies edits AND API rejects the
request.
notes:
Controllers DO own the chart of accounts, fiscal periods, and tax
setup — those are tested separately under their own capabilities.
This case is specifically about tenant identity / company
settings.

title: Controller is allowed to post a journal entry
goal:
Verify that a user signed in as Controller can post a manual journal
entry, and that the action records cleanly in the audit log.
preconditions: A user with the Controller role exists and can sign in.
The chart of accounts is initialized.
At least one open fiscal period exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as the Controller user. Find and open the journal entries
area.
steps.expected:
The journal-entry area is visible and the option to create a
new entry is enabled.

steps.action:
Create a balanced two-line journal entry: debit Cash $100, credit
Owner's Equity $100. Date in the current open period. Post it.
steps.expected:
The entry posts successfully. A confirmation appears with the JE
number.

steps.action:
Open the audit log (or the journal entry's history tab). Find
the entry just posted.
steps.expected:
The audit record names the Controller user, the timestamp, and
the JE number / amounts.

expected_overall: Controller successfully posts a JE and the action is audited.
pass_criteria: JE posted AND audit log shows Controller user AND timestamp AND amounts.

title: Controller is denied posting a payroll run
goal:
Verify a Controller cannot post a payroll run. Although Controllers
post general journal entries, payroll posting is HR-bound — the
segregation prevents finance from unilaterally adjusting employee
compensation.
preconditions: A Controller user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one prepared payroll run exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller. Open the prepared payroll run if visible.
steps.expected:
The run is read-only for this user. The Post action is hidden
or disabled.

steps.action: Attempt the post-payroll endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected with an authorization error.

expected_overall: Controller cannot post payroll.
pass_criteria:
Run not posted AND no payroll GL entries created by this user AND
attempt rejected.
notes:
Some shops grant Controllers a backup post-payroll authority. This
case asserts strict segregation; if your tenant policy differs,
flip the intent and update the manifest.

title: Controller is allowed to void a posted customer invoice
goal:
Verify a Controller can void a posted customer invoice and that the
void reverses the original GL postings while preserving the audit
trail.
preconditions: A Controller user exists.
At least one posted customer invoice exists.
The fiscal period containing the invoice is still open.

steps.action: Sign in as Controller. Open a posted invoice.
steps.expected: Void / cancel action is enabled.

steps.action: Void the invoice. Provide a reason if prompted.

steps.action: Open the audit log for the invoice.
steps.expected:
Both the original posting and the void action remain visible.
The void is attributed to the Controller user with a timestamp
and reason.

expected_overall: Controller voids an invoice; reversal posts and history is preserved.

shows both actions intact.

title: Engineer / R&D is allowed to approve an engineering change request

preconditions: An Engineer / R&D user exists.

steps.action: Approve the ECR. Provide reason or sign-off note if prompted.
steps.expected:
ECR transitions to Approved. Linked BOM / routing changes
transition to Released at the next revision.

steps.action: Open the ECR audit log.
steps.expected:
Approval is attributed to the Engineer with timestamp, sign-off
note, and a snapshot of what was approved.

expected_overall:
Engineer approves an ECR; approval and downstream rev release are
auditable.
pass_criteria:
ECR approved AND linked changes released AND audit log captures
user, timestamp, note, and approved snapshot.

title: Floor Operator is denied posting a journal entry
goal:
Verify a user with only the Floor Operator role cannot reach or
perform manual journal entry posting, neither via UI navigation nor
by addressing the JE area's URL directly.
preconditions: A user assigned only the Floor Operator role exists and can sign in.
The chart of accounts is initialized.
A Controller user previously signed in and noted the URL of the journal-entries area (for the direct-URL check in step 3).

steps.action: Sign in as the Floor Operator. Examine the main navigation.
steps.expected:
The journal-entries / accounting area is either not visible or
is visibly disabled.

steps.action:
Search the application's menus and quick-actions for any way to
reach a journal-entry creation form.
steps.expected: No path leads to a usable JE form.

steps.action: Type the journal-entries URL directly into the address bar.
steps.expected:
The page does not render the JE form. Either the user is
redirected to their role's home page, or a clear "permission
denied" page appears. No journal-entry data is visible on
whatever page does load.

steps.action:
If the application exposes an API, attempt the equivalent
"create journal entry" call with the Floor Operator's session
credentials.
steps.expected:
The request is rejected with a clear authorization error (e.g.,
403). No JE is created.

expected_overall: Floor Operator cannot post a JE through any available surface.
pass_criteria:
No JE is created AND the UI hides or disables the function AND the
direct URL does not reveal data AND any API path returns an
authorization error.
why_this_matters:
This is the most common version of an access-control bug: the UI
hides the action but the backend doesn't enforce, so anyone who
knows the URL can do the action. Verifying both layers catches it.

can do. The first may be HR or IT depending on tenant choice; the
second is always IT / Admin. This case asserts the second.

Employee record vs. system-user account is a key distinction.
Conflating them is a common over-permission failure when HR is
