title: IT Admin is allowed to modify role permissions
goal:
Verify an IT Admin can change a role's permission set, and that
the change is captured in the audit log with prior / new permission
list.
preconditions: An IT Admin user exists.
At least one editable, non-system role exists (e.g., a custom role created during P0).

steps.action:
Sign in as IT Admin. Open role configuration. Open a custom
role and toggle one capability (add or remove a single
permission).
steps.expected:
Change saves. The new permission set is now in effect for users
assigned to that role.

steps.action: Open the role-config audit log.
steps.expected:
Change is attributed to the IT Admin with timestamp, the role
modified, and the prior / new permission list (or diff).

expected_overall: IT Admin modifies role permissions; change is fully auditable.
pass_criteria:
Role permissions updated AND audit log captures actor, timestamp,
role, and before / after permission set.

title: IT Admin is allowed to create a system user
goal:
Verify an IT Admin user can create a new system user, and that the
creation is audited.
preconditions: An IT Admin user exists.
At least one role exists to assign to a new user.

steps.action:
Sign in as IT Admin. Open the user management area. Create a
new user with a test email and a non-admin role.
steps.expected: User is created. Invitation or temporary credential is generated.

steps.action: Open the audit log.
steps.expected:
User creation is attributed to the IT Admin with a timestamp
and the role granted.

expected_overall: IT Admin creates users; action is auditable.
pass_criteria: User created AND audit captures actor, target, role granted, timestamp.

title: IT Admin is allowed to manage API keys and integrations
goal:
Verify an IT Admin can create, rotate, or revoke API keys for
integrations, and that the action is captured with attribution
and key metadata (NOT the secret) in the audit log.
preconditions: An IT Admin user exists.
The integrations / API key area is enabled in tenant config.

steps.action:
Sign in as IT Admin. Open the API keys / integrations area.
Create a new API key with a label and a scoped permission set.
steps.expected:
A new key is generated and shown once. The label and scope are
recorded.

steps.action: Open the integrations audit log.
steps.expected:
Key creation is attributed to the IT Admin with timestamp,
label, scope, and a key fingerprint / id (NOT the secret).

expected_overall: IT Admin manages API keys; action is fully auditable.
pass_criteria:
Key created AND audit log captures actor, timestamp, label, scope,
and key id. The secret value is NOT logged.
notes:
The audit log must never store the API secret in plain text. If
the log shows the full secret, that itself is a finding.

title: IT Admin is denied changing a customer credit limit
goal: Verify an IT Admin user cannot change credit limits.

title: IT Admin is denied modifying tenant identity / company settings
goal:
Verify an IT Admin — who manages users and roles — cannot change

currency). Those settings belong to the Administrator who originally
configured the tenant during P0.
preconditions: An IT Admin user exists with no other roles attached.
Tenant identity is configured.

steps.action:
Sign in as IT Admin. Look for the company / tenant settings
area.
steps.expected:
The company-settings area is either not visible, or visible
read-only with no edit affordances on tenant identity fields.

steps.action: Type the tenant settings URL directly.
steps.expected:
The settings form does not render in editable mode. Permission
denial or read-only view.

steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: IT Admin cannot modify tenant identity.
pass_criteria:
Tenant identity unchanged AND UI denies edits AND direct URL
blocked AND API rejects the request.
notes:
This is a "powerful but bounded" boundary case. IT Admin and
Administrator are easy to conflate; this case asserts the line

title: IT Admin is denied posting a journal entry
goal:
Verify the IT Admin role — which owns user and role management but
no financial authority — cannot post manual journal entries.
preconditions: An IT Admin user exists with no other roles attached.
The chart of accounts is initialized.

steps.action:
Sign in as IT Admin. Look in the navigation for any
journal-entries surface.
steps.expected:
The JE area is not visible, or visible read-only with no create /
post action.

steps.action: Type the journal-entries URL directly into the address bar.
steps.expected:
The page does not render a JE creation form. A permission denial
appears or the user is redirected.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the JE create call with this user.
steps.expected: The request is rejected (e.g., 403). No JE is created.

expected_overall: IT Admin cannot post a JE through any path.
pass_criteria:
No JE created AND UI denies access AND direct URL is blocked AND
API rejects the request.
why_this_matters:
An IT Admin who can also post journal entries can both grant
themselves financial access and act on it — a classic SoD failure.

title: IT Admin is denied posting a payroll run

preconditions: A Maintenance Manager user exists with no other roles attached.

it is read-only with no release action enabled.

steps.expected: The action is rejected with an authorization error.

steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Maintenance Manager cannot release a production WO.
pass_criteria:
No production WO released AND release action not enabled or
rejected AND API rejects the request.
notes:
Maintenance and production work-order workflows often share code
paths; this case verifies that the role split is honored on the
production side specifically.

This is the kind of "powerful but bounded" boundary that often

goal:
Verify a Production Manager — who often originates the demand

No approval recorded AND PO state unchanged AND API rejects the
request.
why_this_matters:
Letting the requester approve their own PO breaks the most basic
spend control in any organization. This case is a high-priority
check whenever a "manager" role gains broad UI access.

steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Production Manager cannot override pricing.
pass_criteria: No override saved AND no override recorded AND attempts rejected.

title: Production Manager is denied posting a journal entry
goal:
Verify the Production Manager role — which has legitimate access
to manufacturing data but no financial authority — cannot post
manual journal entries.
preconditions: A user assigned only the Production Manager role exists and can sign in.
The chart of accounts is initialized.

steps.action:
Sign in as the Production Manager. Look for the journal-entries
area in the navigation.
steps.expected: The JE area is not visible, or is visibly disabled.

steps.action: Type the journal-entries URL directly into the address bar.
steps.expected:
The JE form does not render. A clear authorization message or a
redirect to the user's home page appears.

expected_overall: Production Manager cannot post a JE.
pass_criteria: No JE created AND UI denies access AND direct URL is blocked.
notes:
Production Manager is a common "trusted but not financial" role —
the kind of role where over-permissioning happens because admins
conflate "trusted" with "should have access to everything they're
trusted with." Verifying the boundary holds is worth the case.

title: Production Manager is denied posting a payroll run
goal:
Verify a Production Manager — who approves labor time entries —
cannot post payroll. Time-approval and payroll-post must be
segregated to prevent fraud.
preconditions: A Production Manager user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one prepared payroll run exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Production Manager. Look for any payroll surface.
steps.expected: The payroll surface is not reachable.

steps.action: Attempt the post-payroll endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Production Manager cannot post payroll.
pass_criteria: Run not posted AND attempt rejected.
why_this_matters:
Production Managers approve hours; if they could also post
payroll, they could approve fraudulent time and pay it out
without independent review. The segregation is the control.

title: Production Manager is allowed to release a work order

steps.action: Open the WO history or audit log.
steps.expected:
The release records the user, timestamp, and the BOM/routing
revisions in effect at release.

expected_overall:
Production Manager releases a WO; the release is auditable with
pinned revisions.
pass_criteria: WO released AND audit log captures user, timestamp, and revisions.

title: Sales / Account Manager is denied permanently deleting a customer record
goal:
Verify a Sales user cannot delete customer records, even ones they
themselves created. Soft-deactivate is fine; permanent delete must
require higher authority.
preconditions: A Sales user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer record exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Sales. Open the customer record. Look for a delete
action.
steps.expected:
No delete action is available. A deactivate action may be
offered separately.

steps.action: Attempt the delete endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Sales cannot delete a customer record.
pass_criteria: Customer record unchanged AND endpoint rejected.
why_this_matters:
Sales users own the customer relationship and a frustrated user
may want to "clean up" stale records. Permanent deletion must be
gated above Sales to preserve the audit trail and prevent loss of
history.

title: Sales / Account Manager is denied issuing a customer credit memo
goal:
Verify a Sales user cannot issue credit memos. Sales can request
one (and likely originates the request as part of customer
resolution), but issuance — which posts to GL — is a Controller
action.
preconditions: A Sales user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer with an open AR balance exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Sales. Open the customer's AR.
steps.expected:
A "request credit memo" or draft surface may exist, but the
Issue / post action is hidden, disabled, or replaced with a
handoff to Controller.

steps.action: Attempt the issue-credit-memo endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected with an authorization error.

expected_overall: Sales cannot issue (post) a credit memo.
pass_criteria:
No credit memo posted AND no GL entries created by this user AND
endpoint rejected.
why_this_matters:
Credit memos hit revenue. Letting the customer-facing role post
them — even with good intent — removes the independent financial
check that a credit is justified.

title: Balance sheet ties to AR, AP, inventory, and fixed-asset subledgers
goal:
Run the balance sheet as of the close date and verify that AR, AP,
inventory, and fixed-asset totals match their respective subledger
reports — and that the sheet itself balances.
preconditions: The period has been closed (P5-CLOSE-004).
AR aging, AP aging, inventory valuation, and a depreciation schedule are runnable.

steps.action: Run the balance sheet as of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Total assets and total liabilities + equity are
both displayed.

steps.action: Verify total assets equal total liabilities + equity.
steps.expected: Equal to the cent.

steps.action: AR control = total of AR aging report. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: AP control = total of AP aging report. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Inventory control = total of inventory valuation report. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Net fixed assets = depreciation schedule's net book value total.
Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Balance sheet balances and every control account ties to its
subledger.
pass_criteria:
All four subledger ties match within $0.01 AND assets = liabilities
+ equity.

title: Capacity utilization report reconciles to scheduled WO load and calendar
goal:
Run the capacity utilization report by work center and verify the
load-vs-available numbers reconcile to scheduled WO operation
hours and the calendar's available hours.
preconditions: At least one WO is scheduled (released and dated) at a known work center.
The work center's calendar is configured (P1-CAL-001 or its override per P1-WC-003).

steps.action:
Run the capacity utilization report for the next 7 days, by
work center.
steps.expected:
Report shows available hours and load hours per work center
per day.

steps.action:
For one day, sum the routing-operation hours for every scheduled
WO at that work center. This is the load.
steps.expected: Compute by hand or via a query.

steps.action:
For the same day, the available hours = calendar shift duration
minus any planned-downtime block (P1-CAL-003).
steps.expected: Compute.

steps.action: Compare to the report.
steps.expected: Both load and available match within 0.01 hours.

expected_overall: Capacity report reconciles to scheduled load and calendar.
pass_criteria: Load and available hours both match the hand computation.

title: Cash flow ties to net income and bank-account movement
goal:
Run the cash flow statement (indirect method) and verify it
reconciles to net income and to the actual change in cash on the
balance sheet.
preconditions: The period is closed and a P&L and balance sheet have been run for it.
Beginning and ending cash balances are visible from the balance sheets.

steps.action: Run the cash flow statement for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows operating, investing, and financing sections plus
net change in cash.

steps.action:
Net income reported on the cash flow's first line equals the
P&L's net income.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Net change in cash equals (ending cash on the balance sheet -
beginning cash on the prior balance sheet).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check one investing line (e.g., the asset purchase from
P3-ASSET-COMM-001 should appear under investing).
steps.expected:
Asset purchase shows in investing activities for the correct
amount.

expected_overall:
Cash flow ties to net income and to the balance sheet's cash
movement.
pass_criteria:
All three reconciliations match within $0.01 AND a known investing
transaction is in the right section.

title: Depreciation schedule reconciles to fixed-asset register and GL postings
goal:
Run the depreciation schedule report and verify it ties to the
fixed-asset register and to the period's GL depreciation postings.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset has been commissioned and depreciation has run (P5-CLOSE-003).

steps.action: Run the depreciation schedule report.
steps.expected:
Report lists each asset with cost, accumulated depreciation,
net book value, and current-period depreciation.

steps.action:
For one asset, hand-compute current-period depreciation per its
method (e.g., straight-line: cost / useful life × period).
steps.expected: Compute and compare to the report row.

steps.action:
Sum current-period depreciation across all assets. Compare to
the GL's depreciation expense postings for the period.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum accumulated depreciation. Compare to the accumulated
depreciation contra-asset balance on the trial balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Depreciation schedule ties to per-asset method, GL expense, and
accumulated balance.
pass_criteria: All three reconciliations match within $0.01.

title: Employee labor distribution reconciles to labor postings on WOs
goal:
Run the employee labor distribution report and verify each
employee's total hours equal the sum of their WO labor entries
for the period.
preconditions: At least one employee has recorded labor against WOs in the period (P4-LABOR).

steps.action: Run the employee labor distribution report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per employee: direct hours, indirect hours, total
hours, possibly broken down by work center or WO.

steps.action:
Pick one employee. Pull their labor entries for the period.
Sum the hours.
steps.expected: Total available.

steps.action: Compare to the report's row for that employee.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
Spot-check the breakdown: at least one direct-labor entry on a
WO and (if any) one indirect-labor / stoppage entry should both
appear in the right buckets.
steps.expected: Categorization is correct.

expected_overall: Employee labor distribution ties to underlying labor entries.
pass_criteria:
Per-employee totals match labor entry sum within 0.01 hours AND
direct/indirect categorization is correct.

title: Inventory valuation report ties to the GL inventory account
goal:
Run the inventory valuation report and verify it equals the GL
inventory control account balance to the cent. This is the report
P5-CLOSE-001 leans on.
preconditions: Inventory exists for at least one part.
At least one period has been through the close cycle.

steps.action:
Run the inventory valuation report (system on-hand × cost) as
of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report displays per-part on-hand × unit cost = extended value,
with a grand total.

steps.action:
Run the GL trial balance and read the inventory account net
balance as of the same date.
steps.expected: The balance is visible.

steps.action: Compare grand total to GL inventory balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check one lot-tracked part: sum the open lots' values from
the lot detail. Compare to the part's row total in the report.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Inventory valuation ties to GL and lot detail rolls up correctly.
pass_criteria:
Grand total = GL inventory balance within $0.01 AND the spot-checked
lot rollup matches.

title: MRP exception report identifies real material shortfalls
goal:
Run the MRP exception report and verify it flags only the parts
whose projected supply is short of demand within the planning
horizon, with no false positives or false negatives.
preconditions: At least one open SO with demand exists.
At least one part has demand that exceeds on-hand + on-order minus safety stock.
At least one part has supply ≥ demand (should NOT appear in the exception report).

steps.action: Run the MRP exception report.
steps.expected:
Report lists parts with projected shortage, including: required
date, shortage quantity, suggested action (PO, expedite,
transfer).

steps.action:
Verify the part you know is short appears in the report with
the right shortage quantity.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action: Verify the part you know is fine does NOT appear.
steps.expected: Not present.

steps.action:
Pick one entry. Hand-verify: demand per SO + safety stock vs.
on-hand + on-order. Compute the shortage.
steps.expected: Hand-computed shortage matches the report.

expected_overall:
MRP exception report correctly identifies real shortfalls and skips
non-shortfalls.
pass_criteria:
Known-short part is present AND known-fine part is absent AND
spot-checked shortage matches hand computation.
why_this_matters:
An MRP exception report with false positives gets ignored. One with
false negatives causes stockouts. Both are common bugs and both
silently destroy trust in the planning function.

title: OEE report reconciles to availability, performance, and quality records
goal:
Run the OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) report for a work
center and verify the three component metrics reconcile to source
data: availability from stoppage records, performance from cycle
times, and quality from QC pass / scrap records.
preconditions: At least one work center ran a complete WO with labor, completion, and any QC outcomes recorded.
Stoppage data has been recorded (P5-STOPPAGE) at least once.

steps.action: Run the OEE report for the work center for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows availability %, performance %, quality %, and
composite OEE %.

steps.action:
Availability: (planned production time - stoppage time) /
planned production time. Compute by pulling calendar planned
hours and subtracting recorded stoppage minutes.
steps.expected: Compute. Compare to the report's availability.

steps.action:
Performance: actual cycle output / theoretical output at routing
standard.
steps.expected: Compute. Compare to the report.

steps.action: Quality: good units completed / total units completed.
steps.expected: Compute. Compare to the report.

expected_overall: All three OEE components reconcile to source records.
pass_criteria: Each component matches the report within 0.5 percentage points.
why_this_matters:
OEE is the headline manufacturing metric. If it's wrong, plant
managers either chase ghosts or miss real problems.

title: P&L reconciles to source revenue, COGS, and expense postings
goal:
Run the income statement (P&L) for the closed period and verify that
revenue, cost of goods sold, and operating expense totals tie to the
underlying transactions that produced them.
preconditions: The period has been closed (P5-CLOSE-004).
At least one customer invoice was posted in the period (P4-INV-001).
At least one WO was completed in the period (P4-COMP-FINAL).
At least one vendor invoice was posted (P3-AP-001).

steps.action: Run the P&L for the closed period.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Revenue, COGS, gross margin, operating expense,
and net income are visible.

steps.action:
Total revenue: from the customer-invoice register, sum the
revenue lines posted in the period. Compare to the P&L's revenue.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Total COGS: from the WO completion postings, sum the cost of
goods sold posted in the period. Compare to the P&L's COGS.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Total operating expense: from vendor invoices and JEs posted to
expense accounts in the period, sum. Compare to the P&L total
expense.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
If the P&L supports drill-through, click into a revenue line to
verify the underlying invoice opens.
steps.expected: Drill-through resolves to the source transaction.

expected_overall:
P&L totals tie out to source transaction sums and drill-through
resolves correctly.
pass_criteria:
Revenue, COGS, and expense totals each reconcile within $0.01 to
the source-transaction sum.
why_this_matters:
P&Ls that don't tie out are a leadership-credibility bug. Fixing
the underlying postings is much harder than catching the
reconciliation gap before publishing.

title: Sales by customer report reconciles to invoice register
goal:
Run the sales-by-customer report for the period and verify each
customer's total equals the sum of their posted invoices in the
period.
preconditions: At least one customer invoice exists in the period.

steps.action: Run the sales-by-customer report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report lists each customer with totals (revenue, possibly count
and average).

steps.action:
For one customer, run the invoice register filtered to that
customer for the same period.
steps.expected: Sum of invoices equals the customer's row in the report.

steps.action:
Verify the report's grand total equals the P&L's revenue total
for the same period (less non-customer revenue items if any).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Sales-by-customer ties to the invoice register and the P&L.
pass_criteria:
Per-customer total matches the invoice register AND grand total
ties to the P&L revenue.

title: Sales by product report reconciles to invoice lines
goal:
Run the sales-by-product report for the period and verify each
part's revenue equals the sum of that part's invoice lines.
preconditions: At least one customer invoice with one or more part lines exists in the period.

steps.action: Run the sales-by-product report for the period.
steps.expected: Report lists each part with units sold, revenue, possibly margin.

steps.action:
For one part, query the invoice lines: sum the (qty × unit price
- discount) for that part across all invoices in the period.
steps.expected: Sum equals the report row's revenue.

steps.action:
Grand total of revenue across all products equals the
sales-by-customer report's grand total (and the P&L revenue).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Sales-by-product ties to invoice line detail and to other revenue
reports.
pass_criteria:
Per-part revenue matches invoice line sum AND grand total
reconciles to the P&L.

title: Trial balance shows debits = credits across all accounts
goal:
Run a trial balance for the closed period and verify that total
debits equal total credits, and that each account's balance is
derivable from posted JEs.
preconditions: The period has been closed.

steps.action: Run the trial balance as of the close date.
steps.expected: Report lists every account with debits and credits.

steps.action: Total debits across all accounts should equal total credits.
steps.expected:
Equal to the cent. If not, the books are out of balance — a
severe defect.

steps.action:
Pick one account (e.g., Cash). Sum the JE postings to that
account in the period. Compare to the trial-balance net.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Pick one income-statement account (e.g., Sales Revenue). Verify
the balance equals the sum of revenue postings in the period.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: Trial balance is balanced and each account ties to its postings.
pass_criteria:
Debits = credits AND spot-checked accounts reconcile to JE
postings.

title: Vendor performance report ties to receipt and quality history
goal:
Run the vendor performance report and verify it reflects actual
on-time, on-quality history from receipts and quality records.
preconditions: At least one vendor has multiple receipts (P3-RECV-001) over time.
At least one of those receipts had a partial / damaged quantity (P3-RECV-003).

steps.action:
Run the vendor performance report covering the relevant date
range.
steps.expected:
Report shows per vendor: on-time delivery percentage, defect
rate, fill rate.

steps.action:
For Pacific Steel Supply (or your test vendor), pull the
receipts log. Count: how many receipts were on-or-before the
promised date, how many were full, how many had any rejected
quantity.
steps.expected: Counts available.

steps.action:
Compute: on-time % = (on-time count / total receipts), defect %
= (rejected qty / total received qty), fill % = (received qty /
ordered qty for closed POs).
steps.expected: Numbers computed.

steps.action: Compare to the report.
steps.expected: Match within rounding.

expected_overall: Vendor performance numbers reconcile to receipt and quality records.
pass_criteria: All three computed metrics match the report within rounding.
why_this_matters:
Procurement uses this report to make sourcing decisions. If the
numbers don't reflect reality, vendor management is broken silently.

title: WO variance report reconciles to actual material and labor postings
goal:
Run the WO variance report for closed work orders. Verify the
material and labor variance numbers equal (actual posted - standard
per BOM/routing) for each WO.
preconditions: At least one WO has been completed and closed (P4-COMP-FINAL).

steps.action: Run the WO variance report for closed WOs.
steps.expected:
Report displays standard cost, actual cost, and variance per WO,
broken down by material and labor.

steps.action:
For one closed WO, look up the BOM standard quantities and the
actual material issued (from P4-MATL-ISSUE). Compute (actual -
standard) at unit cost.
steps.expected: Compute by hand or with a spreadsheet. Compare to the report.

steps.action:
Repeat for labor: routing standard hours × labor rate vs. actual
labor recorded (P4-LABOR).
steps.expected: Compute and compare.

steps.action:
Both variance lines on the report should match the hand
calculation within $0.01.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: WO variance report numbers reconcile to source material / labor.
pass_criteria: Material and labor variance match the hand calculation within $0.01.
